findings.title: free_text_reps wrongly generates kg/reps SET rows for the athlete
findings.evidence: detectIntendedUnit flips any letter input to free_text_reps; ExerciseLogCard.tsx:109-124 synthesises numeric kg-labelled SetEntryRows while pure free_text (125-135) uses a merged check/skip cell.
findings.userImpact: A prose prescription shows kg input boxes per set instead of a done/skip.
findings.recommendation: Render free_text_reps like free_text (merged check/skip cell, no kg cell).

verdicts.reasoning:
The code path is real and reachable end-to-end. Traced the full chain:

1. Reachability of unit=free_text_reps: detectIntendedUnit (src/lib/prescriptionParser.ts:26-29) flips ANY letter to free_text_reps, and it is wired into the coach save paths at ExerciseDetail.tsx:216 and PrescriptionGrid.tsx:258. So a coach typing prose with a rep pattern (e.g. "Pause x 3 x 4") saves unit='free_text_reps' with that prescription_raw. Confirmed reachable.

2. setLines is empty for free_text_reps: In useWeekPlans.ts writePrescription, line 530 `const hasNumericLines = parsed.length > 0 && !isNonNumeric && !isFreeTextReps` is false when isFreeTextReps, so `lines=[]` (541) and replaceSetLines(id, []) writes ZERO planned_set_lines rows. trainingLogService.ts:283-314 then loads setLines straight from those rows, so planned.setLines.length === 0 at runtime. This means the ExerciseLogCard.tsx:107 branch is skipped and the cited 109-124 fallback DOES fire.

3. Manifestation in the card: ExerciseLogCard.tsx:109-124 synthesises one SetRowInput per parsed free-text line WITHOUT setting freeTextMode (plannedLoadText = coach prose e.g. "Pause", plannedRepsValue numeric, plannedLoadValue null). In SetEntryRow.tsx:228, `input.freeTextMode` is falsy, so it renders the two-column grid (lines 252-275) with a kg NumericCell and an r NumericCell — NOT the merged check/skip prose cell that pure free_text/other gets (ExerciseLogCard.tsx:125-135 sets freeTextMode:true). So the athlete is shown per-set kg input boxes (placeholder = the prose, "Pause") for a prose prescription. Exact match to the finding's evidence and userImpact.

The cited file:line (ExerciseLogCard.tsx:109-124) and the contrast with 125-135 are accurate. The StackedNotation header already renders free_text_reps as prose (StackedNotation.tsx:13-14), so the kg cells below are redundant/confusing for a prose-load prescription.

Severity adjusted high → medium: it is a genuine, reachable translation/UX defect, but (a) it is not data corruption — plannedLoadValue is null so nothing is wrongly stored as a kg target, and load saves null until the athlete types a figure; (b) the coach in this branch DID author reps and sets ("x 3 x 4"), so generating quantified set rows is partly defensible (free_text_reps means "numeric reps with prose load"); the kg cell, not the existence of rows, is the off note. Note the user's most egregious example — pure prose with NO rep pattern ("moderate work") — does NOT hit this branch: parseFreeTextPrescription returns [] so no rows are synthesised (a separate dead-card outcome), which is a distinct finding. This finding specifically covers prose-load-with-reps showing kg boxes, which is a real but lower-impact UX issue than the high label implies.
verdicts.correctedEvidence: Reachability confirmed via useWeekPlans.ts:530 (hasNumericLines excludes isFreeTextReps ⇒ lines=[] ⇒ zero planned_set_lines) and trainingLogService.ts:311-314 (setLines loaded from those rows ⇒ empty). ExerciseLogCard.tsx:109-124 then synthesises rows without freeTextMode, so SetEntryRow.tsx:252-275 renders kg+reps NumericCells (placeholder=prose) instead of the merged check/skip prose cell at SetEntryRow.tsx:228-251 used by free_text/other (set via ExerciseLogCard.tsx:132 freeTextMode:true).

verdicts.reasoning:
The observed behaviour is real but the framing/recommendation are wrong, and severity is inflated.

WHAT'S TRUE: ExerciseLogCard.tsx:109-124 synthesises rows for free_text_reps WITHOUT freeTextMode, so SetEntryRow falls into the numeric grid branch (SetEntryRow.tsx:252-276). The load cell is a NumericCell hard-labelled "kg" (SetEntryRow.tsx:256 placeholder={input.plannedLoadText}, line 321 unit="kg", NumericCell at 292-324), with the coach's prose ("moderate", set at ExerciseLogCard.tsx:117 plannedLoadText: line.loadText) shown only as a greyed placeholder. So a textual-load prescription does present a "kg" input box. That mismatch is genuine.

WHY THE RECOMMENDATION IS UNSOUND: free_text_reps is NOT semantically equivalent to free_text. It carries STRUCTURED reps × sets — the coach-side dropdown literally labels it "Free text with reps × sets" (constants.ts:13) and the rows carry plannedRepsText/plannedRepsValue from parseFreeTextPrescription (ExerciseLogCard.tsx:116-119; parser at prescriptionParser.ts:190-210). StackedNotation renders it as load-over-reps columns (StackedNotation.tsx:121-156), confirming reps are first-class. Collapsing it into the free_text merged ✓/✗ cell (the fix proposed) would DESTROY per-set reps logging — a regression that conflicts with coach-flexibility (the coach deliberately chose a unit that quantifies reps) and single-source-of-truth. The correct fix keeps the reps cell and only de-"kg"s the load cell (render the coach's prose as a read-only/text cell, not a kg NumericCell).

USER-CONCERN MISMATCH: the user's verbatim "free text should NOT generate a set" is about PURE free_text, which is ALREADY handled correctly via freeTextMode merged ✓/✗ (ExerciseLogCard.tsx:125-135). free_text_reps is a distinct unit and was not the user's example.

SEVERITY: "high" is inflated. The athlete still gets working per-set reps entry plus done/skip; the only defect is a "kg" label on a load box whose placeholder is prose — a cosmetic translation mismatch, no data loss and no dead card on the parse-success path. Medium.

Separately, a genuine dead-card bug exists for free_text_reps when prescription_raw lacks any × (parseFreeTextPrescription → [] → base stays empty, no fallthrough to the free_text branch because line 125 is else-if → "No set lines defined" at ExerciseLogCard.tsx:332-335), but that is a DIFFERENT finding than the one under test (which is about generated kg/reps rows).
verdicts.correctedEvidence: ExerciseLogCard.tsx:109-124 builds free_text_reps rows without freeTextMode; the "kg" mislabel is in SetEntryRow.tsx:256 (placeholder) + 321 (unit="kg") via NumericCell (292-324). The reps cell at SetEntryRow.tsx:262-274 is correct and must be preserved; only the load cell should become a non-kg/read-only text cell. Fix is NOT to mirror the free_text merged check/skip cell (ExerciseLogCard.tsx:125-135 / SetEntryRow.tsx:228-251), which would drop structured reps.

findings.dimensionOverview: Top bugs in prescription-to-athlete translation.

findings.title: free_text_reps with no parseable reps is a dead card the athlete cannot complete or skip
findings.evidence: Prose with no separator (Technique work) becomes free_text_reps with no set lines; rows is empty; card shows No set lines defined with no check/skip, no Mark-complete; expectedPlannedSetCount returns null so never auto-promotes.
findings.userImpact: Athlete cannot acknowledge or skip; session never completes; coach sees not-logged.
findings.recommendation: Fall back to the free_text merged check/skip row and return 1 in expectedPlannedSetCount.

verdicts.reasoning:
I traced the full pipeline and every link of the chain holds.

PRODUCTION OF THE DATA (reachable): In ExerciseDetail.applyText (src/components/planner/ExerciseDetail.tsx:212-219) a coach's free-form text runs through detectIntendedUnit. detectIntendedUnit (src/lib/prescriptionParser.ts:26-37) strips x/X/×/* then, if ANY letter remains, returns 'free_text_reps'. So prose like "Technique work" / "Mobility" / "Recovery" (no x/× separator) is stored with unit='free_text_reps' and prescription_raw="Technique work". This is a normal, common coaching input — highly reachable.

NO SET LINES STORED: writePrescription (src/hooks/useWeekPlans.ts:529-542) computes hasNumericLines = parsed.length>0 && !isNonNumeric && !isFreeTextReps. Because isFreeTextReps is true, hasNumericLines is ALWAYS false for free_text_reps, so lines=[] and replaceSetLines is called with an empty array (which only trims, never inserts, lines 475-486). Therefore planned_set_lines is guaranteed empty for any free_text_reps exercise → planned.setLines.length===0 on the athlete side.

DEAD CARD: In ExerciseLogCard.tsx the rows useMemo (94-140): line 107 setLines.length>0 is false; line 109 unit==='free_text_reps' && prescription_raw is true, so it calls parseFreeTextPrescription("Technique work"). parseFreeTextSegment (prescriptionParser.ts:190-209) splits on /\s*[x×]\s*/ — with no separator parts.length===1, matching neither the ===2 nor ===3 branch, returning null → parseFreeTextPrescription returns []. The for-loop body never runs, base stays []. The free_text/'other' fallback at line 125 does NOT fire (unit is free_text_reps, not free_text). So rows=[]. At line 332 rows.length===0 renders only the "No set lines defined for this exercise" message (333-335). The Log-as-prescribed/Mark-complete block (338-359), the SetEntryRow list, and the "Add set" button (476-484) are ALL inside the else branch (337-485) and do not render. The only control outside the ternary is the notes textarea (488-503) — a note cannot mark the exercise done or skipped.

NEVER COMPLETES: expectedPlannedSetCount (src/lib/plannedSetCount.ts:21-24) returns null for this case (parseFreeTextPrescription→[]→length 0→return null). The auto-promote check in TodayScreen (src/athlete/v2/screens/TodayScreen.tsx:557-558) only runs from inside onSaveSet, which can never fire because no SetEntryRow is rendered. And isExerciseDone (src/lib/trainingLogModel.ts:84-89) returns false when le.sets.length===0. So the exercise can reach 'completed' only via path 1 (explicit Mark-complete) — which is exactly the control that is not rendered here. Net: the athlete cannot complete OR skip; the coach sees not-logged. Confirmed.

Severity adjustment: I lowered critical→high. It is a genuine reachable functional dead-end with real user impact, but it does not corrupt data, crash, or block the rest of the session (other exercises on the day log normally), and it requires the coach to author separator-less prose. That is common but is a single-exercise stuck state, not a system-wide critical. The cited files/lines and the recommended fix (fall back to the free_text merged ✓/✗ row and return 1 from expectedPlannedSetCount) are accurate.
verdicts.correctedEvidence: Mechanism fully confirmed. Root cause is two-fold and both cited lines are correct: (1) src/hooks/useWeekPlans.ts:530 forces hasNumericLines=false for free_text_reps, guaranteeing empty planned_set_lines; (2) ExerciseLogCard.tsx:109-124 parses prescription_raw but parseFreeTextPrescription returns [] when there is no x/× separator (prescriptionParser.ts:194-209), so base/rows stay empty and the dead-card branch at ExerciseLogCard.tsx:332-335 renders. plannedSetCount.ts:23 returns null. The free_text/'other' fallback at ExerciseLogCard.tsx:125-135 deliberately does NOT cover free_text_reps, which is the gap.

verdicts.reasoning:
Verified end-to-end and the finding holds.

REACHABILITY (real, high-frequency, not theoretical): In ExerciseDetail.tsx:216-219, free-form prose typed by a coach is routed through detectIntendedUnit(textValue). prescriptionParser.ts:28-29 strips x/X/×/* then returns 'free_text_reps' for ANY remaining letter. So reps-less OWL cues ("Technique work", "Mobility", "Max effort", "AMRAP") are saved as unit='free_text_reps' with prescription_raw set, with NO automatic path to 'free_text' (free_text is only reachable via the explicit Unit dropdown at ExerciseDetail.tsx:716-721). parseFreeTextSegment (prescriptionParser.ts:190-209) splits on the x-pattern; prose with no separator yields parts.length===1 → returns null → parseFreeTextPrescription returns []. 

DEAD CARD CONFIRMED: In ExerciseLogCard.tsx:94-140 the rows useMemo is an else-if chain: the free_text_reps branch (109-124) matches and runs the loop over an empty lines array, leaving base=[]; the free_text/'other' ✓/✗ fallback (125-135) is therefore never reached. rows.length===0 hits the dead branch at 332-335 ("No set lines defined"). Log-as-prescribed is gated on unit==='absolute_kg' (239), and Mark-complete is gated on loggedSets.length>0 (349) — both unavailable. There is genuinely no ✓, ✗, or complete control.

AUTO-PROMOTE: plannedSetCount.ts:21-24 returns null (lines.length===0). But this is the secondary blocker; the primary is that isExerciseDone (trainingLogModel.ts:86) returns false when le.sets.length===0, and no set can ever be created because there's no row. The auto-promote in TodayScreen.tsx:555-566 only runs inside the save-set handler, which never fires. Coach review shows "not logged" with no recourse in the card.

FIX SOUNDNESS: The recommendation is correct AND complete — it correctly names BOTH halves. Returning 1 in expectedPlannedSetCount alone is insufficient (no row → no set → isExerciseDone still false); the load-bearing half is synthesising the free_text merged ✓/✗ row. That row shape (freeTextMode:true, freeTextPlanned:prescription_raw) already exists and is exercised for free_text/'other' (ExerciseLogCard 125-135, SetEntryRow 114-135, 228-251), and is already correctly suppressed from the trash control (ExerciseLogCard 374-382), so the fix reuses an established pattern with no new architecture and no token/theme concern (athlete app is intentionally dark). No EMOS principle is violated: prose stays read-only planned data; the athlete writes a separate log set with performedText=null and status=skipped/completed — exactly the "accepted, or marked did-not-do, NOT a generated quantified set" behaviour the user asked for. Implementer must mirror the same empty-parse fallback in expectedPlannedSetCount so the two stay in sync (the file header explicitly states it mirrors the card). 

SEVERITY: critical is defensible — complete, no-workaround functional dead-end on a high-frequency input class, and it cascades into session-never-completes plus a false "not logged" to the coach. It sits at the critical/high boundary (no data corruption/crash; athlete can still leave session-level notes), but the total loss of in-card interaction across a broad, natural set of coach inputs warrants critical.
verdicts.correctedEvidence: Primary reachability is ExerciseDetail.tsx:216-219 (detectIntendedUnit routes reps-less prose to free_text_reps) + prescriptionParser.ts:190-209 (parseFreeTextSegment returns null without an x). Dead-card branch: ExerciseLogCard.tsx:94-140 (else-if chain skips the free_text ✓/✗ fallback) → 332-335. Auto-promote: plannedSetCount.ts:21-24 returns null, but the deeper blocker is isExerciseDone (trainingLogModel.ts:86) with sets.length===0 — so the row fallback, not just returning 1, is the essential fix.

findings.dimensionOverview: Top bugs in prescription-to-athlete translation.

findings.title: percentage and rpe render as kg cells; a one-tap check logs the number as a kg load
findings.evidence: expandSetLines is unit-agnostic so a percent or RPE value shows in a cell labelled kg; a bare check back-fills plannedLoadValue as kg; the per-row check is unguarded.
findings.userImpact: Tapping check records the percent/RPE number as kg; coach delta and tonnage ingest a bogus value.
findings.recommendation: Make expandSetLines unit-aware and suppress the back-fill for non-absolute_kg units.

verdicts.reasoning:
Reachability fully substantiated end-to-end for the actively-offered `percentage` unit (constants.ts:10-15 lists percentage in DEFAULT_UNITS; rpe/other are legacy but still render and exist in the DB).

Write path (useWeekPlans.ts writePrescription, 491-546): for unit='percentage', isNonNumeric=false and isFreeTextReps=false, so it runs parsePrescription(prescription). parsePrescription STRIPS the '%' (prescriptionParser.ts:61 `.replace(/%/g,'')`), so "80%×3×5" parses to {load:80,reps:3,sets:5} and hasNumericLines=true (line 530). It then writes planned_set_lines with load_value:80 (line 537) — the bare percent number. The same numeric branch applies to unit='rpe' (rpe is NOT isNonNumeric and NOT isFreeTextReps), so an "8×3" rpe row stores load_value:8.

Render path (ExerciseLogCard.tsx): planned.setLines.length>0 ⇒ expandSetLines(planned.setLines) (line 108). expandSetLines (SetEntryRow.tsx:330-352) is unit-agnostic — it has no unit parameter and produces plannedLoadValue=line.load_value (=80) and plannedLoadText="80". isFreeTextUnit (ExerciseLogCard.tsx:157) is true ONLY for free_text/other, so percentage/rpe fall into the numeric branch of SetEntryRow (252-275), rendering NumericCell with a hard-coded unit="kg" label (line 257). SetEntryRow cannot be unit-aware: SetRowInput (interface lines 15-32) carries no unit field.

Back-fill (SetEntryRow.tsx:133-141, cited correctly): tapping the ✓ check with empty inputs sets completing=true, so performedLoad = parsedLoad ?? input.plannedLoadValue = 80, with no unit guard. commit() builds the payload with performedLoad:80 and plannedLoad:input.plannedLoadValue:80 (lines 149-157). These persist to training_log_sets.performed_load / planned_load (trainingLogService.ts:729-731) as kg. So one tap records 80% (or RPE 8) as an 80 kg / 8 kg load.

Coach impact confirmed: LoggedStackedNotation (StackedNotation.tsx:263-298) renders performed_load with NO % indicator, and compareToPlan (196-201) computes deltas treating both numbers as kg; any tonnage = performed_load×reps ingests the bogus value.

StackedNotation.tsx:165-168 (cited) is correct supporting context, not the bug site: it shows the parser yields a numeric load and the '%' is re-appended ONLY for the header display (`${line.load}${unit==='percentage'?'%':''}`), proving the unit IS known at render time yet the athlete's editable cell drops it. The actual defect lives in expandSetLines/SetEntryRow (unit-blind) + ExerciseLogCard's isFreeTextUnit gate that excludes percentage/rpe.

Evidence and recommendation (make expandSetLines unit-aware; suppress back-fill for non-absolute_kg) are accurate. High severity is justified: silent data corruption on a one-tap path for an actively-offered unit.
verdicts.correctedEvidence: Bug site: SetEntryRow.tsx:133-141 (unguarded back-fill) + SetEntryRow.tsx:330-352 (expandSetLines has no unit param) + SetEntryRow.tsx:252-275/257 (hard-coded unit="kg") + ExerciseLogCard.tsx:157 (isFreeTextUnit only covers free_text/other, so percentage/rpe route to numeric kg cells). Root cause that percent stored as numeric load_value: useWeekPlans.ts:529-542 (parsePrescription strips '%' at prescriptionParser.ts:61). StackedNotation.tsx:165-168 is supporting context (header re-appends '%', proving unit is known) rather than the defect location.

verdicts.reasoning:
The bug is real, reachable, and the impact is accurate; I tried to refute it and could not.

ROOT CAUSE confirmed end-to-end:
1. percentage/rpe prescriptions DO produce planned_set_lines with load_value = the percent/RPE number. In useWeekPlans.ts:529-541 writePrescription, isNonNumeric = free_text|other and isFreeTextReps = free_text_reps, so percentage and rpe fall into hasNumericLines; parsePrescription strips "%" (prescriptionParser.ts:61) so "80%x3x3" stores load_value=80, and an RPE like "8x3x3" stores load_value=8 (there is no rpe-specific parse path; StackedNotation.tsx:88-178 only special-cases "%" for percentage and never handles rpe, so rpe is treated as a plain numeric load).
2. expandSetLines (SetEntryRow.tsx:330-352) is unit-agnostic: line 346 sets plannedLoadValue = line.load_value, and the loadText (lines 336-339) is the bare number with no "%". ExerciseLogCard.tsx:125-157 only sets freeTextMode for free_text/other, so percentage/rpe render the numeric kg/reps cells (SetEntryRow.tsx:252-275) whose unit suffix is hardcoded "kg" (line 256). So an 80% set displays placeholder "80" + "kg".
3. The unguarded one-tap back-fill is real: SetEntryRow.tsx:133-135 — completing = status==='completed'; performedLoad = parsedLoad ?? (completing ? input.plannedLoadValue : null). Tapping the check (setDone, line 170-174) with an empty load commits performedLoad = 80 (the percent). performedReps back-fills from plannedRepsValue (line 137-141), a real rep count >0.
4. handleSaveSet (TodayScreen.tsx:518-542) stores patch.performedLoad straight into performed_load via upsertLoggedSet with NO unit guard — unlike handleLogAsPrescribed which DOES guard unit!=='absolute_kg' (TodayScreen.tsx:591). This asymmetry confirms the maintainers already know absolute_kg-only back-fill is the rule.
5. Downstream corruption confirmed: analysis factFetch.ts:432-452 hardcodes unit:'absolute_kg' for performed facts ("performed loads are always kg") and computes tonnage = performed_load * reps with loadIsKg:true and maxLoad = performed_load. Meanwhile the PLANNED side resolveLoad correctly yields kgLoad:0 for percentage-without-refmax and for rpe (factFetch.ts:181-202). So the back-filled 80 becomes 80 kg of performed tonnage and an 80 kg max — a value the plan never counted. The performed-fact filter (status==='completed' && performed_reps>0, line 437) is satisfied because the check back-fills reps, so the path is fully reachable. PR detection also ingests valueKg: patch.performedLoad (TodayScreen.tsx:577), so the bogus 80 can even fire a false PR.

SEVERITY: stays high, not inflated. It is silent data corruption of performed tonnage/maxLoad plus a possible false PR, on two of the most common OWL prescription types (% and RPE), triggered by the most natural athlete gesture (tap the check). It is bounded only by the athlete leaving the load blank; typing real kg avoids it. That matches the user's verbatim concern that prescription types don't "translate well to the athlete."

FIX SOUNDNESS: the recommendation is correct and EMOS-consistent. Suppressing back-fill for non-absolute_kg mirrors the existing handleLogAsPrescribed guard and the planned-vs-logged separation principle; it won't affect absolute_kg (unchanged) or free_text/other (already freeTextMode). Making expandSetLines unit-aware fixes the "kg" mislabel. One refinement worth noting (does not lower the verdict): the true ingestion point is factFetch.ts:433/441-452 treating any performed_load as kg, and the same "kg" mislabel also exists on the COACH surface (CoachSetEditModal.tsx:315-318 hardcodes "kg"), so a complete fix should also stop the analysis layer from counting non-absolute_kg performed loads as kg and relabel the coach editor cell.
verdicts.correctedEvidence: Back-fill: SetEntryRow.tsx:133-135 (completing back-fills performedLoad = plannedLoadValue), saved unguarded via TodayScreen.tsx:518-542 (no unit guard, vs the guard at TodayScreen.tsx:591). unit-agnostic expansion: SetEntryRow.tsx:330-352 (esp. 346) + hardcoded "kg" cell at SetEntryRow.tsx:256 and bare-number loadText at SetEntryRow.tsx:336-339 — this, not StackedNotation.tsx:165-168 (which correctly renders "%"), is the mislabel source; same hardcoded "kg" on coach side at CoachSetEditModal.tsx:315-318. Origin of the value: useWeekPlans.ts:529-541 stores percent/RPE in load_value. Downstream corruption: factFetch.ts:432-452 treats performed_load as kg (tonnage/maxLoad) while planned side correctly zeroes it at factFetch.ts:181-202.

findings.dimensionOverview: Top bugs in prescription-to-athlete translation.

findings.title: Deleting a second set inside the 4s undo window silently abandons the first delete (data loss / orphaned set)
findings.evidence: handleDeleteSet optimistically removes the set from local state and stores ONE pending deletion in `pendingSetDelete` (a single object, not a queue): `setPendingSetDelete({ setId, set: setRow })` (TodayScreen.tsx:698). The actual DB delete only happens in UndoToast.onDismiss after the 4000ms timeout (TodayScreen.tsx:1152-1160). Reproduction: athlete taps trash on set A (A vanishes from UI, pendingSetDelete=A, toast shows). Within 4s they tap trash on set B. handleDeleteSet(B) runs `setPendingSetDelete({ setId: B, set: setRowB })`, OVERWRITING pendingSetDelete=B. The UndoToast `visible` prop stays true the whole time, so the effect's cleanup (clearTimeout) + re-arm fires only because `onDismiss`/`pendingSetDelete` identity changes — but A's setId is now lost. When the timer finally fires it calls deleteLoggedSet(B) only. Result: set A is gone from the UI (optimistically removed) but its DB row was NEVER deleted, and on next loadDay it reappears — OR, depending on render timing, B is the one resurrected. Either way the athlete's intent and the stored truth diverge, and the coach sees a set the athlete believed they removed.
findings.userImpact: Athlete deletes two sets in quick succession; one of them comes back on the next refresh (or the wrong one stays deleted). Trust in the logger erodes and the coach reviews a session that does not match what the athlete did.
findings.recommendation: Make the pending-delete a queue (array) keyed by setId, or commit the previous pending delete immediately when a new delete arrives before replacing it (flush-on-overwrite). Simplest robust fix: in handleDeleteSet, if a pendingSetDelete already exists, synchronously commit it (void runSave(()=>deleteLoggedSet(prev.setId))) before setting the new one. The single-toast UI can still show only the latest.

verdicts.reasoning:
Verified against the real code. `pendingSetDelete` is a single nullable object, not a queue (TodayScreen.tsx:126-130). `handleDeleteSet` (TodayScreen.tsx:678-699) optimistically removes the set from local `data` state and unconditionally calls `setPendingSetDelete({setId, set})` — there is NO guard preventing a second delete while one is pending, and NO flush of the previous pending delete. The only DB-commit path for a pending delete is `UndoToast.onDismiss` (TodayScreen.tsx:1152-1160), which calls `deleteLoggedSet(setId)` for whatever `pendingSetDelete` currently holds. Confirmed via Grep that this line 1158 is the sole call site of `deleteLoggedSet` in the athlete app — no unmount/save/flush path exists.

The UndoToast timer mechanism (UndoToast.tsx:44-54) re-arms correctly across the overwrite: its effect deps are [visible, onDismiss, timeoutMs]; `onDismiss` is an inline arrow in JSX (TodayScreen.tsx:1152) so it gets a fresh identity on every render. When delete B arrives, the component re-renders with `pendingSetDelete=B`; the effect cleanup clears A's timer and re-arms a fresh 4000ms timer whose `onDismiss` closure captures B. A's `deleteLoggedSet` is therefore never called. Since `loadDay`/`fetchAthleteDay` (TodayScreen.tsx:182-211) re-fetches all sets from the DB, set A — optimistically removed from the UI but never deleted in the DB — reappears on the next refresh. The coach then reviews a set the athlete believed they removed. Reachability confirmed: handleDeleteSet is wired to onDeleteSet on ExerciseLogCard (TodayScreen.tsx:1009, 1028) and triggered by athlete taps on delete buttons (ExerciseLogCard.tsx:378-379, 440-442; OffPlanExerciseCard.tsx:133).

One precision correction to the finding's evidence: the outcome is deterministic, not ambiguous. The finding hedges 'OR, depending on render timing, B is the one resurrected.' That is not accurate — the timer always commits the LATEST pending delete (B), so B is correctly deleted and the EARLIER delete (A) is always the abandoned/resurrected one. The 'either way the truth diverges' conclusion still holds and severity is unaffected; only the non-determinism framing is overstated. Severity high is appropriate: silent data divergence in the core logging surface, reachable by a normal double-delete gesture within a 4s window, eroding coach/athlete trust.
verdicts.correctedEvidence: Mechanism confirmed at TodayScreen.tsx:678-699 (handleDeleteSet overwrites single pendingSetDelete with no flush/guard) and TodayScreen.tsx:1124-1161 (sole commit via UndoToast.onDismiss → deleteLoggedSet of latest pending only). UndoToast.tsx:44-54 re-arms the timer because onDismiss is a new inline-arrow identity each render. Correction: the abandoned set is deterministically the EARLIER delete (A), not 'either A or B'; A's DB row is never deleted and resurrects on the next fetchAthleteDay/loadDay (TodayScreen.tsx:182-211).

verdicts.reasoning:
Verified against the actual code. `pendingSetDelete` is a SINGLE object, not a queue (TodayScreen.tsx:126-130). handleDeleteSet optimistically removes the set from local state and stores exactly one pending deletion via `setPendingSetDelete({ setId, set: setRow })` (TodayScreen.tsx:684-698). The real DB delete only fires in UndoToast.onDismiss after the timeout, calling `deleteLoggedSet(setId)` for whatever the LATEST pendingSetDelete holds (TodayScreen.tsx:1152-1160).

The timer mechanics confirm the abandonment. UndoToast's effect has deps `[visible, onDismiss, timeoutMs]` (UndoToast.tsx:46-54). `visible` = `pendingSetDelete != null` stays true across an A→B transition (never passes through null), and `onDismiss` is an inline arrow recreated on every render of TodayScreen (TodayScreen.tsx:1152). So when the second delete calls setPendingSetDelete(B) and re-renders, the effect cleanup runs clearTimeout on A's timer and re-arms a fresh 4000ms timer whose onDismiss closure now captures pendingSetDelete=B. When it fires it calls deleteLoggedSet(B) only. A was already removed from the UI optimistically (TodayScreen.tsx:685-697) but deleteLoggedSet(A) is NEVER called — A's timer was cleared and never re-armed for A. On the next loadDay, set A's surviving DB row reappears. This is reachable with two ordinary taps inside the 4s window — no exotic race needed.

One correction to the evidence's hedge: the outcome is deterministic, not render-timing-dependent. It is always the FIRST-deleted set (A) that resurrects, because the timer always re-arms on the latest pending value and only ever commits the latest; B is correctly deleted. The reviewer's "OR ... B is resurrected" alternative does not occur, but this does not weaken the bug.

Severity: high is defensible and I keep it, though it sits at the high/medium boundary. The failure mode is a MISSED delete (the set resurrects and is recoverable by re-deleting), not irreversible destruction of data — no planned data is mutated, consistent with EMOS planned-vs-logged separation. But the athlete's intent diverges from stored truth and the coach reviews a session that does not match what the athlete believes they logged, which the user explicitly flagged as the robustness concern. High is appropriate.

Fix soundness: the recommended flush-on-overwrite (synchronously commit the prior pending delete via runSave(() => deleteLoggedSet(prev.setId)) before storing the new one) is correct, minimal, and EMOS-aligned. It is confined to the athlete dark surface (no design-token concerns), does not fork prescriptionParser/StackedNotation, and only affects deletion of an already-logged set row — so it cannot break other prescription types (free_text, percentage, combo, sentinels) or the coach surface. It preserves the single-toast UX (the prior delete simply loses its undo affordance the instant the next delete arrives, which matches a single-toast model). The queue alternative is also valid. Confirmed.

findings.dimensionOverview: The athlete write path is unusually careful for a mobile logger: primitive-dep effects keep mid-edit local state from being stomped on parent re-renders (SetEntryRow, RawScoreDial, GppLogCard), set rows are upserted on the stable (log_exercise_id, set_number) key with new rows numbered strictly max+1 (collision-safe across deletes/gaps), the per-row chainRef serialises a blur-then-status double-fire, GPP uses a coalescing serial queue to kill the historic duplicate-row race, ensureLogExercise tolerates legacy duplicates via order+limit(1), and runSave deliberately avoids reloading on transient network errors so it does not unmount cards mid-edit. Planned-vs-performed separation is respected everywhere (removePlannedSet/GPP/substitution all write to training_log_*, never the plan). Those are genuine strengths. However there are several REAL reachable problems where the athlete loses data or gets stuck: (1) the single-slot pending-set-delete drops the previous delete when a second set is deleted inside the 4s undo window — the first set is gone from the UI but never deleted server-side, OR the reverse depending on timing; (2) the free_text_reps dead card with no parsable "x" leaves an exercise with zero rows and no way to mark it done; (3) every onBlur-only textarea (session notes, exercise notes, GPP exercise-name) silently loses the last edit if the app is closed/backgrounded without blurring; (4) the bonus-day-delete reads a stale `overview` closure and double-sets dayIndex; (5) read-modify-write of session.custom_metrics and exercise.metadata is non-atomic, so concurrent metric/GPP writes can clobber keys; (6) save failures surface only as a small red banner the athlete will not see while scrolled down a long session, with no per-field retry. Findings below are ordered by severity.

findings.title: free_text_reps prescription with no parsable 'x reps' is an un-loggable dead card
findings.evidence: When unit==='free_text_reps' and there are no planned_set_lines, ExerciseLogCard re-parses prescription_raw with parseFreeTextPrescription (ExerciseLogCard.tsx:109-124). parseFreeTextSegment only returns a row when the segment splits on x/× into 2 or 3 parts with a numeric reps part (prescriptionParser.ts:194-205); a coach who types just 'Technique work' (which detectIntendedUnit forces to free_text_reps because of the letters, prescriptionParser.ts:29) yields ZERO parsed lines. base=[] → rows=[] → the card renders the static text 'No set lines defined for this exercise' (ExerciseLogCard.tsx:332-334) with no ✓/✗ row, no Add-set button (Add-set is inside the rows>0 branch), and no Mark-complete. expectedPlannedSetCount also returns null for this case (plannedSetCount.ts:21-24), so auto-promotion never fires either. The athlete literally cannot record that they did or skipped this exercise.
findings.userImpact: A perfectly valid coach instruction ('Easy technique work', 'Mobility', 'Whatever feels good') becomes an inert card the athlete can neither tick, skip, nor acknowledge — the session shows as incomplete and the coach gets no signal at all.
findings.recommendation: When unit==='free_text_reps' but parseFreeTextPrescription returns [], fall through to the SAME single merged ✓/✗ row that free_text/'other' uses (ExerciseLogCard.tsx:125-135), carrying prescription_raw as freeTextPlanned. Mirror the change in expectedPlannedSetCount (return 1 in that branch) so auto-promote stays consistent. This is the minimal fix that turns the dead card into the 'accept / did-not-do-it' control the user explicitly asked for.

verdicts.reasoning:
Every link in the reachability chain is substantiated against the real code.

1. Coach reaches unit=free_text_reps with unparseable raw: ExerciseDetail.tsx:216-219 runs detectIntendedUnit(textValue) on the free-form prescription box and persists the detected unit. prescriptionParser.ts:28-29 strips x/X/×/* then returns 'free_text_reps' for ANY remaining letter. So typing "Technique work" / "Mobility" / "Whatever feels good" flips the unit to free_text_reps and stores the prose in prescription_raw.

2. No structured set lines are stored for free_text_reps: useWeekPlans.ts:530 forces hasNumericLines=false when isFreeTextReps, so lines=[] (line 541) and replaceSetLines(plannedExId, []) is called (line 542). replaceSetLines (lines 472-486) skips the upsert and deletes all rows with position>0, leaving planned_set_lines empty. Confirmed planned.setLines.length===0 in the athlete card.

3. Re-parse yields zero rows: ExerciseLogCard.tsx:107-124 — setLines empty + unit free_text_reps → parseFreeTextPrescription(prescription_raw). parseFreeTextSegment (prescriptionParser.ts:190-209) only returns a row when split on /\s*[x×]\s*/gi gives 2-3 parts AND the reps part parseInt is a non-negative number. "Technique work" has no x → parts.length===1 → returns null → lines=[] → base=[] → rows=[] (after the removedSetNumbers filter at line 139). Note this is even broader than the finding states: prose containing an 'x' but no trailing integer reps (e.g. "Box jumps", "Max effort", "Flexibility") also splits but fails the parseInt check and is equally dead.

4. Dead card confirmed: ExerciseLogCard.tsx:332-335 renders only the static "No set lines defined for this exercise" when rows.length===0. The Log-as-prescribed / Mark-complete block (336-359), the set rows + Add-set button (361-484) are ALL inside the rows.length>0 else-branch, so none render. Mark-complete is additionally gated by loggedSets.length>0 (line 349). Only the notes textarea (488-503) survives. No ✓/✗, no Add-set, no acknowledgement.

5. Auto-promotion cannot fire: plannedSetCount.ts:21-24 returns null for free_text_reps with empty parse. TodayScreen.tsx:557-558 calls isExerciseDone(projectedLe, null). trainingLogModel.ts:84-89: le.sets.length===0 short-circuits to false (line 86) — and there is no way to ever create a set, so the exercise stays permanently incomplete and the session never registers it as done. Coach gets no signal.

All cited file:line references are accurate. This is a fully reachable runtime bug triggered by a routine, valid coach action, and it directly matches the user's verbatim concern that free-text exercises should be acceptable/skippable rather than generating broken state. High severity stands.

Traced the full chain end-to-end and every link holds.

REACHABILITY (real, ordinary coach flow): detectIntendedUnit (prescriptionParser.ts:28-29) strips x/X/×/* then returns 'free_text_reps' on ANY remaining [a-zA-Z]. ExerciseDetail.tsx:216-218 applies that detected unit on save. So a coach typing prose like "Technique work" / "Mobility" / "Whatever feels good" into the free-form prescription field lands on unit='free_text_reps'. writePrescription (useWeekPlans.ts:529-542) then sets hasNumericLines=false for free_text_reps (the `!isFreeTextReps` guard on line 530) and writes replaceSetLines(…, []) — i.e. NO planned_set_lines are stored for free_text_reps. Confirmed: planned.setLines.length===0 in the card.

DEAD CARD (confirmed at cited lines): In ExerciseLogCard rows useMemo (lines 94-140), setLines empty → free_text_reps branch (line 109) → parseFreeTextPrescription('Technique work'). parseFreeTextSegment (parser.ts:190-209) splits on /\s*[x×]\s*/gi; a prose string with no x/× yields parts.length===1 → returns null → lines=[] → base=[] → rows=[]. Render falls into the rows.length===0 branch (lines 332-335) showing the static "No set lines defined for this exercise" with NO ✓/✗ row, and critically the Add-set button (476-484) and Mark-complete (349-357) are BOTH inside the rows.length>0 else-branch, so neither renders. The athlete genuinely cannot tick, skip, or acknowledge the exercise.

AUTO-PROMOTE (claim correct; mechanism slightly imprecise): expectedPlannedSetCount returns null for this case (plannedSetCount.ts:21-24). The reviewer says auto-promote "never fires" because of the null. The more precise reason: isExerciseDone (trainingLogModel.ts:84-89) does `count = plannedSetCount ?? le.sets.length`, and with zero sets created it returns false at line 86 regardless of the null. Same outcome (never completes); the cited consequence is correct.

FIX SOUNDNESS (sound, low risk): The recommended fallback — when free_text_reps parses to [], synthesise the SAME merged freeTextMode row that free_text/'other' uses (ExerciseLogCard.tsx:125-135) carrying prescription_raw as freeTextPlanned — is verified safe against SetEntryRow: a freeTextMode row with freeTextPlanned set renders the prose (SetEntryRow.tsx:230-233), exposes ✓/✗ (197-226), and on commit writes performedLoad:null/performedReps:null/performedText:null (lines 114-126) — i.e. it does NOT generate a numeric set, exactly satisfying the user's "accept / did-not-do-it, NOT a set" requirement. isPlannedFreeTextRow trash-suppression (ExerciseLogCard.tsx:374-382) already keys off freeTextMode+freeTextPlanned, so it applies automatically. The fix is additive (a fallback inside the existing free_text_reps branch), so the parsable free_text_reps path ("Easy x 5"), structured set-lines, combos, and all other units are untouched. Mirroring expectedPlannedSetCount to return 1 in that branch keeps auto-promote consistent. Minor implementer note: isFreeTextUnit (line 157) is still free_text||other only, so the Log-as-prescribed/Mark-complete gate at line 338 will treat the new row like a normal row — harmless since canLogAsPrescribed is false for free_text_reps and the ✓/✗ on the row is the real control.

SEVERITY: high is accurate — reachable via the normal coach UI, blocks the athlete entirely, produces zero coach signal, and is a direct instance of the user's verbatim concern. Not inflated. Reachability is a subset of free_text_reps usage (prose with no parsable "x reps" and no stray 'x' letter that would split), but that subset is common and the failure is total, so high holds rather than medium.

findings.title: onBlur-only persistence silently loses the last note/GPP-name edit when the app is backgrounded or closed without blurring
findings.evidence: Every textarea persists ONLY on blur: session notes (SessionHeader.tsx:154 onBlur), per-exercise notes (ExerciseLogCard.tsx:193 and :493), off-plan notes (OffPlanExerciseCard.tsx:163), GPP notes (GppLogCard.tsx:218). The GPP exercise-name AutoGrowExerciseInput does fire onChange→updateRow per keystroke (GppLogCard.tsx:180), so it is safe; but all the free-text NOTE fields are not. On mobile the dominant way a session ends is the user switching apps, locking the phone, or the PWA being evicted — none of which fires a blur on the focused textarea. The typed-but-unblurred note is lost. There is no visibilitychange/pagehide flush and no beforeunload handler anywhere in the athlete app (grep shows none). Unlike numeric set cells, notes are the highest-value qualitative data the coach reads.
findings.userImpact: Athlete types 'left shoulder felt off, stopped at 80%' in session notes, gets a call, locks the phone. The note is gone forever; the coach never learns why the session was short.
findings.recommendation: Add a flush-on-hide: register a `visibilitychange`/`pagehide` listener (in TodayScreen or a small hook) that, when document becomes hidden, commits any dirty note fields. Lightest-touch alternative: also commit notes on a short debounce while typing (e.g. 800ms idle) in addition to onBlur, reusing the existing onChange handlers. Either respects the API-first boundary (still routes through onPatchNotes/onUpdateNotes).

verdicts.reasoning:
All cited code paths are real and reachable, and every line reference checks out. Every free-text NOTE field in the athlete app persists ONLY on blur, with onChange merely updating local state: SessionHeader.tsx session-notes textarea (onChange=setNotes at :153, onBlur→onPatchNotes at :154-158); ExerciseLogCard.tsx sentinel-note textarea (:189-200, onBlur→onUpdateNotes at :192-196) and the main per-exercise note textarea (:490-502, onBlur at :493-497); OffPlanExerciseCard.tsx note textarea (:160-171, onBlur at :163-167); GppLogCard.tsx note textarea (:214-226, onBlur at :218-222). None of these commit on change or on a debounce.

The finding's contrast that the GPP exercise-name input is safe is also correct: GppLogCard.tsx:178-181 AutoGrowExerciseInput fires onChange→updateRow(:115-119)→enqueueSave→drainQueue→onSave per keystroke, so row fields (name/reps/sets/load/done) persist continuously; only the GPP note textarea is onBlur-only.

The flush-mechanism claim is verified by grep over src/athlete: there is NO beforeunload, pagehide, or unload listener anywhere. The two existing visibilitychange listeners are unrelated to note flushing — AthleteLayout.tsx:86-89 only refreshes the inbox unread count when the tab becomes visible, and CoachThreadScreen.tsx:62-70 only reloads message threads. Neither commits dirty note/textarea state. So on a hard background/lock/PWA-eviction, no blur fires on the focused textarea and no fallback flush exists; the typed-but-unblurred note is lost. This is a real, reachable data-loss path, not theoretical: mobile app-switch/lock does not reliably emit blur before the page is frozen/discarded, and notes are coach-facing qualitative data with no other capture.

One correction to the finding's framing (does not change the verdict): the claim "Unlike numeric set cells, notes are not [committed]" is imprecise — numeric kg/reps cells in SetEntryRow.tsx are ALSO onBlur-commit (:239 commit on blur for the merged free-text cell; :310 onBlur=onCommit for the numeric cells). Only the ✓/✗ status buttons commit immediately. So the same onBlur-only data-loss risk extends to typed-but-unblurred kg/reps values too; the note fields are the highest-value instance but not the only one. Severity stays high: silent loss of the highest-value qualitative field under a common mobile usage pattern, with zero flush safety net.
verdicts.correctedEvidence: Confirmed line refs: SessionHeader.tsx:153-158, ExerciseLogCard.tsx:191-196 and 491-497, OffPlanExerciseCard.tsx:161-167, GppLogCard.tsx:216-222. No beforeunload/pagehide/unload in src/athlete (grep). The two visibilitychange listeners (AthleteLayout.tsx:86-89, CoachThreadScreen.tsx:62-70) only refresh inbox count / reload threads — neither flushes dirty notes. Note: SetEntryRow.tsx numeric cells are also onBlur-commit (:239, :310), so the data-loss surface is slightly broader than notes alone; only ✓/✗ status taps persist immediately.

verdicts.reasoning:
All five cited textareas are onBlur-only and the cited lines are exact. Session notes: SessionHeader.tsx:151-162 commits only in onBlur (line 154-158) via onPatchNotes, which wires to TodayScreen.tsx:401 handlePatchNotes → patchSession({session_notes}) (immediate Supabase write, no debounce). Per-exercise notes (sentinel branch ExerciseLogCard.tsx:189-200 line 192; normal branch :490-502 line 493), off-plan notes (OffPlanExerciseCard.tsx:160-171 line 163), and GPP notes (GppLogCard.tsx:214-226 line 218) all gate the void onUpdateNotes(notes) call inside onBlur, routing to TodayScreen.tsx:624/663 updateLogExercise({performed_notes}). None debounce on type.

The finding's claim that the GPP exercise-NAME field is safe is verified: GppLogCard.tsx:180 AutoGrowExerciseInput fires onChange→updateRow (line 115-119)→enqueueSave→drainQueue (line 87-113), persisting every keystroke via a serial queue. So GPP rows (name/reps/sets/load/done) are immune; only the GPP free-text NOTE textarea shares the onBlur-only flaw. The finding correctly scoped this.

The decisive claim is verified by grep: the ONLY visibilitychange listeners in the athlete app (AthleteLayout.tsx:87-96, CoachThreadScreen.tsx:63-67) fire on becoming VISIBLE to reload data (if (!document.hidden) void load()), not on becoming hidden to flush. There is no pagehide/beforeunload/document.hidden-flush anywhere. So a textarea that holds focus when the phone is locked, the app is backgrounded, or the PWA is evicted never blurs → the last note is lost. On mobile/iOS Safari this background/lock path is genuinely common and does not reliably fire blur, so the data-loss is reachable, not theoretical.

Severity high is justified: notes are the highest-value qualitative field the coach reads, the loss is silent, and the trigger (type last thought → lock phone) is a normal mobile pattern. It is bounded to a single unblurred free-text field per surface (numeric sets and GPP rows persist on their own immediate paths), so it is not a catastrophic loss-everything bug — but high is appropriate for silent loss of irrecoverable coach-facing data. The recommended fix is sound and EMOS-consistent: a visibilitychange/pagehide flush (or an additive debounce-on-type alongside the existing onBlur) still routes through onPatchNotes/onUpdateNotes, preserves the planned-vs-logged separation and API-first boundary, and cannot break any other prescription type since it only adds an extra commit trigger for the same dirty-check the onBlur already performs. The debounce alternative is the lighter touch and equally safe.

findings.title: Save failures are reported only by a small top-of-page banner the athlete cannot see while logging mid-session; no per-field error or retry
findings.evidence: runSave sets `error` on failure and surfaces it once, at the very top of the screen (TodayScreen.tsx:912-917), above the WeekNavigator/DayChipRow/SessionHeader and all exercise cards. A long session is many screens tall, so a set save that fails (e.g. RLS rejection, transient 5xx, expired token) while the athlete is scrolled down to exercise 8 shows no visible feedback there. The SetEntryRow's own `busy` flag clears in `finally` regardless of success/failure (SetEntryRow.tsx:160-167), so the row looks saved (spinner stops, value stays in the local input) even though it was rejected. For non-transient errors runSave then calls loadDay() (TodayScreen.tsx:295-297) which silently reverts the optimistic value to the server's (unsaved) state — the athlete's typed number just disappears with no explanation near the field.
findings.userImpact: On a flaky connection or after a token expiry the athlete keeps tapping ✓ and typing loads, sees no error, then on a later refresh discovers half the session is blank — with no idea which writes failed or how to retry.
findings.recommendation: Surface save failures at the point of action: keep the failed value in the field and show an inline retry affordance on the affected SetEntryRow/card (the chainRef already serialises, so a per-row 'retry' that re-commits the snapshot is feasible). At minimum, make the global error banner a fixed/sticky toast and DON'T silently revert via loadDay on a write failure when the user's local value is the more recent intent.

verdicts.reasoning: Every claim is substantiated by the real code. (1) Error surface: `error` is a single shared state (TodayScreen.tsx:108) rendered only once, at TodayScreen.tsx:912-917, inside the normal `space-y-4` flow (root div at :883) ABOVE the WeekNavigator (:884), DayChipRow (:900) and all ExerciseLogCards (:988-1018). It is NOT fixed/sticky (no fixed/sticky class anywhere — grep on :912-917 confirms plain block), so on a tall session it scrolls out of view. (2) runSave (TodayScreen.tsx:278-301) catches the error, calls setError, and resolves WITHOUT rethrowing. handleSaveSet (:518-581) is just `runSave(...)`, so the promise SetEntryRow awaits never rejects. (3) In SetEntryRow.commit (SetEntryRow.tsx:160-167) `busy` is set true then cleared in `finally` after awaiting `mine` (which resolves even on failure), so the row's spinner stops, the local input value stays, and the row looks saved. I read the entire SetEntryRow render (lines 185-289): there is NO per-row error indicator or retry affordance — only the busy/disabled state. (4) On a non-transient error runSave calls loadDay() (:295-297); loadDay (:182-211) refetches server state into `data`, and SetEntryRow's useEffects (:78-92) re-sync local inputs from `logged`, so the athlete's typed-but-rejected value is silently reverted to the unsaved server value with no near-field feedback. Reachability is real: a save failure requires an actual rejection (RLS reject, transient 5xx, expired token, or stale-state error) — not the happy path — but the upsert/update service calls genuinely can throw, so the path is reachable. Medium severity is correct: not the common case, but real data-loss-without-feedback when it occurs, directly matching the user's robustness concern. Worth noting as a small correction/aggravation: the banner header text is hardcoded \"Failed to load\" (TodayScreen.tsx:914), so a SAVE failure shows misleading copy claiming a load error.
verdicts.correctedEvidence: All cited lines are accurate. Supporting context: shared `error` state at TodayScreen.tsx:108 (used by both loadDay and runSave); handleSaveSet at TodayScreen.tsx:518-581 returns runSave(...) so failures never reject up to SetEntryRow; SetEntryRow re-sync useEffects at SetEntryRow.tsx:78-92 are what revert the field after loadDay; banner header is hardcoded "Failed to load" at TodayScreen.tsx:914 (misleading on a save failure).

findings.title: Bonus-day deletion reads a stale `overview` closure and double-sets dayIndex, risking landing on the just-deleted day
findings.evidence: handleDeleteBonusDay.onConfirm does `await deleteSession(sessionId); await loadWeek(); setDayIndex(prev => overview?.days.find(d => d.dayIndex !== prev)?.dayIndex ?? null)` (TodayScreen.tsx:774-779). `overview` here is the value captured when the confirm callback was created — the PRE-delete overview that still lists the deleted bonus day. So `overview.days.find(d => d.dayIndex !== prev)` searches the stale list (which still contains the deleted day) and may resolve to a day that no longer matches the freshly-loaded week. Worse, loadWeek itself already calls setDayIndex via pickDefaultDay (TodayScreen.tsx:166-169) because `prev` (the deleted day) is no longer in the new `w.days`; this manual setDayIndex then races/overrides that with a value derived from stale data. Best case it works by luck; worst case the screen tries to load a deleted slot and shows an empty/erroring day.
findings.userImpact: After deleting an extra training day, the athlete can be dropped onto a blank or wrong day, or briefly see a load error, instead of cleanly landing on a remaining valid day.
findings.recommendation: Don't read the stale `overview` closure. Let loadWeek's own pickDefaultDay (which runs against the fresh fetch) choose the next slot, and drop the manual setDayIndex entirely; or have loadWeek return the new overview and compute the next dayIndex from THAT value.

verdicts.reasoning:
The closure-staleness mechanism is real, but the headline impact is refuted.

REAL part: At TodayScreen.tsx:777-779 the manual `setDayIndex(prev => overview?.days.find(d => d.dayIndex !== prev)?.dayIndex ?? null)` reads `overview` from `handleDeleteBonusDay`'s render closure. `handleDeleteBonusDay` is a plain function recreated each render (not memoized), so its `overview` const is the PRE-delete value; the `setOverview(w)` inside `loadWeek` (line 165) does not mutate that already-captured const. So yes, the manual updater operates on the stale, pre-delete day list. And yes, `loadWeek` already calls `setDayIndex` internally (line 166-169) — since the deleted bonus day is no longer in fresh `w.days`, that internal updater falls through to `pickDefaultDay(w)`. The manual updater then runs second and overrides it. This double-set + stale-closure read is a genuine code smell, and the recommendation (drop the manual setDayIndex and let loadWeek's pickDefaultDay own the choice) is sound.

REFUTED part — the asserted user impact ("dropped onto a blank or wrong day", "tries to load a deleted slot", "shows an empty/erroring day", "risking landing on the just-deleted day") does NOT manifest:
1. Cannot land on the deleted day: the predicate `d => d.dayIndex !== prev` explicitly EXCLUDES `prev` (the deleted day). It can never return the deleted dayIndex.
2. Cannot land on an invalid/absent day in the reachable case. This handler only deletes BONUS days. Per trainingLogService.ts:427-447, the overview's days are active_days (from plan config) + bonus days (days with a session). `deleteSession` (line 798) deletes exactly one session by id, so deleting a bonus day removes exactly one day from the overview. Bonus days are appended AFTER active days (allDayIndices = [...activeDays, ...bonusDays], line 432), so the stale list's FIRST element is an active day. `find(d => d.dayIndex !== deletedBonus)` returns that first active day — which is unaffected by session deletion and therefore still present in the fresh week. So the manual updater resolves to a valid surviving day. Even with zero active days + multiple bonus days, the find returns a surviving bonus day; deleting the last/only bonus day yields `?? null`, matching loadWeek's own `pickDefaultDay`→null.
3. No erroring/blank intermediate fetch: both setDayIndex calls run synchronously in the same microtask continuation after `await loadWeek()` resolves, so React batches them and the loadDay effect (dep [athlete, weekStart, dayIndex], line 211/214) fires once with the FINAL (manual) value, which is a valid day.

So the only true defect is a minor UX one: the manual updater redundantly overrides loadWeek's smarter pickDefaultDay heuristic (today / first-unfinished / first) with a dumber stale-derived "first non-deleted day", so the athlete may land on a valid-but-less-ideal day. That is low severity, not medium, and the "blank/wrong/deleted/erroring day" framing is unsubstantiated by the real data model and code paths.
verdicts.correctedEvidence: TodayScreen.tsx:777-779 — manual `setDayIndex` reads the pre-delete `overview` closure and redundantly overrides loadWeek's own setDayIndex (TodayScreen.tsx:166-169). Because bonus days are appended after active days (trainingLogService.ts:432) and the find predicate excludes the deleted day, the result is always a VALID surviving day — just not necessarily the pickDefaultDay heuristic's preferred one. No deleted/blank/erroring day occurs.

findings.dimensionOverview: The athlete write path is unusually careful for a mobile logger: primitive-dep effects keep mid-edit local state from being stomped on parent re-renders (SetEntryRow, RawScoreDial, GppLogCard), set rows are upserted on the stable (log_exercise_id, set_number) key with new rows numbered strictly max+1 (collision-safe across deletes/gaps), the per-row chainRef serialises a blur-then-status double-fire, GPP uses a coalescing serial queue to kill the historic duplicate-row race, ensureLogExercise tolerates legacy duplicates via order+limit(1), and runSave deliberately avoids reloading on transient network errors so it does not unmount cards mid-edit. Planned-vs-performed separation is respected everywhere (removePlannedSet/GPP/substitution all write to training_log_*, never the plan). Those are genuine strengths. However there are several REAL reachable problems where the athlete loses data or gets stuck: (1) the single-slot pending-set-delete drops the previous delete when a second set is deleted inside the 4s undo window — the first set is gone from the UI but never deleted server-side, OR the reverse depending on timing; (2) the free_text_reps dead card with no parsable "x" leaves an exercise with zero rows and no way to mark it done; (3) every onBlur-only textarea (session notes, exercise notes, GPP exercise-name) silently loses the last edit if the app is closed/backgrounded without blurring; (4) the bonus-day-delete reads a stale `overview` closure and double-sets dayIndex; (5) read-modify-write of session.custom_metrics and exercise.metadata is non-atomic, so concurrent metric/GPP writes can clobber keys; (6) save failures surface only as a small red banner the athlete will not see while scrolled down a long session, with no per-field retry. Findings below are ordered by severity.

findings.title: Non-atomic read-modify-write of session.custom_metrics and exercise.metadata can clobber concurrent edits
findings.evidence: setSessionCustomMetric SELECTs custom_metrics, mutates the JS object, then UPDATEs the whole jsonb (trainingLogService.ts:1665-1683). removePlannedSet and setLogExerciseGppSection do the same whole-object read-modify-write on training_log_exercises.metadata (trainingLogService.ts:623-648, 659-674). These are not atomic. handlePatchCustomMetric runs each metric write through runSave but runSave does not serialise across DIFFERENT handlers — two custom-metric fields committed in rapid succession (athlete fills two fields, second blur fires before the first round-trip returns) each read the pre-mutation snapshot, so the second write overwrites the first key. Same hazard if the coach (Log mode) and athlete both touch metadata/custom_metrics on the same session, or an athlete edits a GPP row while a removePlannedSet for a sibling exercise is in flight (different rows → safe) — but two custom metrics on one session is a normal single-user scenario. CLAUDE.md's last-write-wins guarantee is per-RECORD, not per-jsonb-KEY, so this violates the intent for keyed data.
findings.userImpact: Athlete enters two custom metrics (e.g. 'sleep hours' then 'soreness') back-to-back; one of the two silently does not persist. Coach sees a half-filled metric row and assumes the athlete skipped it.
findings.recommendation: Make these writes key-atomic. For Postgres, use jsonb_set via an .rpc or an UPDATE expression so only the touched key is modified server-side (e.g. custom_metrics = jsonb_set(coalesce(custom_metrics,'{}'), '{<defId>}', <value>)). If staying client-side, serialise all metadata/custom_metrics writes for one session through a single in-flight queue (like GppLogCard's drainQueue) so reads always see the latest committed value.

verdicts.reasoning:
All cited code paths are reachable and the race is real. setSessionCustomMetric (trainingLogService.ts:1665-1683) does a blind whole-jsonb read-modify-write: SELECT custom_metrics → spread into next → UPDATE custom_metrics with no version/updated_at precondition. removePlannedSet (623-648) and setLogExerciseGppSection (659-674) do the identical whole-metadata read-modify-write. runSave (TodayScreen.tsx:278-301) does NOT serialize: it only toggles a `saving` flag and awaits the passed fn; there is no shared queue/mutex, so two runSave invocations run concurrently. handlePatchCustomMetric (405-411) routes each metric write through runSave, and CustomMetricField commits on blur via `void onChange(...)` (CustomMetricField.tsx:24-39, 57) — fire-and-forget, not awaited. SessionHeader renders one independent CustomMetricField per enabled definition (SessionHeader.tsx:138-145), so two adjacent metric fields writing the same session.custom_metrics jsonb is a normal single-user flow. If field B's SELECT lands before field A's UPDATE commits, B reads the pre-A snapshot and overwrites A's key — silent loss with no error. CLAUDE.md's last-write-wins is per-record, not per-jsonb-key, so this violates the intent for keyed data. Strong corroboration that this is a recognized hazard: GppLogCard.tsx:75-113 explicitly built a serial drainQueue to eliminate exactly this "parallel calls race" for GPP saves, while the custom-metric and removePlannedSet/metadata paths have no equivalent protection.

Honest reachability caveat (why medium, not higher): for a single athlete the two-field clobber is intermittent, not deterministic — the athlete normally types into field B in the interval between the two blur commits, which usually (but not always) lets field A's UPDATE commit before field B's SELECT. The window widens with rapid Tab/Enter navigation (Enter blurs the field, CustomMetricField.tsx:58), with the multi-writer coach-Log-mode + athlete scenario the finding describes, and on slow/mobile networks. The recommendation (server-side jsonb_set via RPC, or a per-session serial queue mirroring GppLogCard.drainQueue) is the correct fix. The removePlannedSet-vs-sibling case the finding itself flags as "different rows → safe" is correctly excluded; the real exposures are two keys in one jsonb column (custom_metrics, or gpp+other keys in one exercise's metadata, or two removePlannedSet calls on the SAME log_exercise).

findings.title: Auto-promote and PR check read stale `data` closure, so completion/PR can lag or miss when set saves overlap
findings.evidence: handleSaveSet builds the projected set list and the PR check from `data?.log?.exercises.find(...)` captured at the time the handler closure was created (TodayScreen.tsx:547, 554, 569). Because mergeLoggedSet/mergeLogExercise update `data` via setState (async, not reflected in this same closure), when an athlete completes the last two planned sets in quick succession the second handleSaveSet still sees `currentSets` WITHOUT the first set's new status. The comment at 544-546 claims it 'avoids stale-closure issues' by projecting from currentSets, but currentSets is itself read from the stale closure `data`. Net effect: isExerciseDone (TodayScreen.tsx:558) can under-count terminal sets and skip the auto-promote to 'completed' until a later save/refresh; similarly the PR exercise def lookup (line 569) can miss a just-substituted exercise. Not data loss — the set itself is saved correctly via upsertLoggedSet — but the derived completion state and PR prompt are unreliable under rapid tapping.
findings.userImpact: Athlete ticks the last sets fast; the exercise (or whole session) doesn't flip to 'done' until they touch it again or reload, and a genuine PR prompt may not appear.
findings.recommendation: Compute the projected set list from a ref or from the value returned by the merge functions rather than the closure `data`. Either keep a mutable ref mirroring `data.log.exercises`, or have mergeLoggedSet return the updated logExercise so handleSaveSet can use that authoritative value for isExerciseDone and the PR check.

verdicts.reasoning: The stale-closure MECHANISM is confirmed, but the stated USER IMPACT is mostly refuted. Confirmed: handleSaveSet (TodayScreen.tsx:547, 554, 569) reads `data` from the closure captured at render time, and there is no parent-level serialization — runSave (278-301) just toggles `saving` and runs fn with no queue. SetEntryRow has only a PER-ROW `busy`/`chainRef` (SetEntryRow.tsx:73, 98, 161-167); two distinct set rows are separate component instances, and `globalSaving` (the `saving` flag) is passed to ExerciseLogCard but NOT threaded down to SetEntryRow's buttons (ExerciseLogCard.tsx:384-392, 427-469 pass only readOnly), so a fast double-tap on the last two set rows can fire both handleSaveSet calls before the first set's mergeLoggedSet setData re-renders. In that window the second closure's `currentSets` (line 547) excludes set 1, so isExerciseDone(projectedLe, plannedCount) (line 558, with predicate at trainingLogModel.ts:80-90 requiring terminal>=count) under-counts and the auto-promote to log.status='completed' (lines 559-563) is skipped. So the training_log_exercises.status column genuinely lags. HOWEVER, the claim that "the exercise (or whole session) doesn't flip to done" is REFUTED: every athlete-facing completion indicator derives from set statuses, not from log.status. ExerciseLogCard's DoneChip uses `allCompleted = completedCount >= rows.length` from loggedSets (ExerciseLogCard.tsx:147-148, 269); SessionPreview uses `logged.sets.every(s => s.status==='completed')` (SessionPreview.tsx:227-228); both reflect the correctly-merged set data even during the race. Whole-session done is a separate MANUAL action (handleFinishSession, TodayScreen.tsx:829-840), not the per-exercise auto-promote. The PR claim is only partially valid and via a NARROWER condition than stated: the PR exercise-def lookup (line 569) can fall back to planned.exerciseDef, but in the common no-substitution case that fallback IS the correct exercise so the prompt still fires; a genuine miss requires a just-made substitution not yet merged, not mere rapid tapping. The PR check's performed values come from `patch` and prHistory from the useCallback dep (lines 569-580, 444-491), neither of which is stale. Net: a real latent stale-closure bug that leaves the DB exercise-status column lagging (could matter to coach-side/analytics reads of training_log_exercises.status), but the athlete-facing UI impact asserted in the finding is largely masked. Low severity is correct.
verdicts.correctedEvidence: Mechanism is real at TodayScreen.tsx:547/554/558/569 (stale `data` closure + no parent-level save serialization; SetEntryRow's `busy` is per-row and `globalSaving` is not threaded to set rows, so overlapping saves are reachable). But the user-visible effect is the lagging training_log_exercises.status DB column, NOT a stuck "done" indicator: ExerciseLogCard.tsx:147-148/269 and SessionPreview.tsx:227-228 derive completion from set statuses (correctly merged) rather than log.status. The PR-miss is limited to the just-substituted-then-immediately-logged case (line 569 fallback to planned.exerciseDef), not generic rapid tapping.

findings.title: BodyweightField / CustomMetricField silently discard invalid input on blur with no feedback
findings.evidence: BodyweightField.commit reverts the input to the prior value when the parse is non-finite or <= 0 (BodyweightField.tsx:24-28) with no error message. CustomMetricField.commit calls onChange(null) — clearing the stored value — when a number field's input is non-finite (CustomMetricField.tsx:31-34). So an athlete who fat-fingers '8o' (letter o) into bodyweight, or types a stray character into a numeric custom metric, sees their entry vanish on blur and (for the custom metric) any previously-saved value is wiped to null. There is no inline validation hint.
findings.userImpact: Athlete believes they logged their bodyweight/metric; the value disappeared because of a typo, and they have no idea why or that a previously stored value was cleared.
findings.recommendation: On parse failure, keep the user's raw text in the field and show a brief inline 'enter a number' hint rather than silently reverting/clearing. For CustomMetricField specifically, do NOT onChange(null) on a parse failure (that destroys a valid prior value) — only clear when the field is genuinely empty.

verdicts.reasoning:
Both cited code paths are real, reachable, and behave exactly as the finding describes. BodyweightField.tsx:16-29: commit() runs on onBlur (line 42); when text is non-empty and parses to a non-finite value or <=0, the else branch (lines 26-28) silently reverts text to the prior value (toFixed(1) of value) with no error message. Because the input is type="text" with inputMode="decimal", a fat-fingered "8o" is freely typed and then vanishes on blur. Confirmed.

CustomMetricField.tsx:24-39: for a value_type==='number' metric, when local is non-empty (line 25 guard passes) but parseFloat is non-finite (e.g. a stray letter), commit() calls onChange(null) at line 32. I traced the wiring: SessionHeader.tsx:143 onChange={value => onPatchCustomMetric(def.id, value)} -> TodayScreen.tsx:405 handlePatchCustomMetric -> setSessionCustomMetric (trainingLogService.ts:1660). At trainingLogService.ts:1674 `if (value === null) delete next[definitionId];` and the result is persisted via update(). So a typo on a metric that already had a valid stored value genuinely DELETES and persists the removal of that value, with no inline feedback. This is real data loss, not a theoretical concern, and it is reachable precisely because the field is type="text" so non-numeric characters reach commit(). The empty-string path (line 25) clearing to null is intended behavior; the destructive path requires non-empty-but-unparseable input, which is exactly the typo scenario.

Cited line ranges all match the actual source. I am raising severity from low to medium: the BodyweightField half is a low-severity silent-revert UX issue, but the CustomMetricField half silently destroys a previously-saved valid value on a single mistyped character with zero feedback — that is unannounced persisted data loss, which warrants medium. The recommendation is sound: keep raw text + show an inline "enter a number" hint on parse failure, and in CustomMetricField do not onChange(null) on parse failure (only clear when genuinely empty).
verdicts.correctedEvidence: BodyweightField.tsx:24-28 (revert-on-failure) and CustomMetricField.tsx:31-34 (onChange(null) on non-finite). The destructive persistence is confirmed downstream at src/lib/trainingLogService.ts:1674 (`if (value === null) delete next[definitionId]`), reached via SessionHeader.tsx:143 -> TodayScreen.tsx:405 handlePatchCustomMetric.

findings.title: Sentinel (TEXT/IMAGE/VIDEO) cards have no acknowledge affordance, so a session can never read as fully done
findings.evidence: Sentinel text/image/video cards render the note plus a notes textarea but expose no ✓/✗ or Mark-complete (ExerciseLogCard.tsx:179-204). expectedPlannedSetCount returns null for sentinels (plannedSetCount.ts:29), so they never auto-promote and are never counted as 'done'. The athlete CAN still Finish the session (TodayScreen.tsx:1041) so they are not hard-stuck, but a coach instruction-card the athlete read and followed leaves no positive 'seen/done' signal, and the per-card completedCount/rows badge shows nothing for it. Combined with the fact that WeekScreen's 'X/Y sessions done' counts session.status, not exercise completeness, this is cosmetic rather than blocking — but it is an asymmetry an expert user notices.
findings.userImpact: Athlete reads a coaching cue / watches the prescribed video but has no way to confirm 'got it'; the coach can't tell whether the cue was seen.
findings.recommendation: Add an optional lightweight 'Got it' toggle on sentinel cards that writes status='completed' on the log_exercise (no set row), and have it contribute to the done count. Low effort, reuses updateLogExercise. Keep it optional so it never blocks Finish.

verdicts.reasoning: All cited conditions hold against the real code. (1) ExerciseLogCard.tsx:179-204 is an early return for sentinelType text/image/video that renders ONLY SentinelDisplay + a notes textarea — no checkmark, no Mark-complete, no onMarkComplete wiring (the function returns before the set-entry/Mark-complete UI at lines 338-358). The textarea onBlur only persists performed_notes, never a status. (2) plannedSetCount.ts:29 returns null for sentinels (empty setLines; unit is not free_text_reps/free_text/other → falls through). (3) Auto-promotion in TodayScreen.tsx:557-565 is gated on isExerciseDone(); for a sentinel le.sets.length === 0 so isExerciseDone (trainingLogModel.ts:86) returns false — confirming sentinels can never auto-promote and the only path to status='completed' (explicit Mark complete, line 85) is absent from the sentinel card. (4) Athlete is not hard-stuck: Finish session (TodayScreen.tsx:1041-1050) is always available, gated only on session.status and saving. (5) Verified the finding's WeekScreen claim: WeekScreen.tsx:78 counts d.status==='completed' (session-level), not exercise completeness, and :161 renders the X/Y counter — so the gap is cosmetic and does not propagate to the week counter. Minor wording nuance: the per-card completedCount/rows badge (ExerciseLogCard.tsx:320-321) lives in the NON-sentinel branch, so a sentinel card never renders that badge at all rather than rendering it empty — accurate either way. Net: a real, reachable UX asymmetry (coach gets no 'seen' signal for instruction/video cards; athlete has no 'got it' affordance) but correctly non-blocking with no data-integrity impact, so low severity stands.
