findings.title: Custom metric value_type cannot be changed after creation; correcting a mistype orphans history
findings.evidence: The create form lets the coach choose number|text (WeekMetricsSettings.tsx:415-422), but the edit form (handleEditSave, WeekMetricsSettings.tsx:217-237) only patches `label` and `unit` — value_type is never in the edit payload. updateMetricDefinition's type signature does accept value_type (trainingLogService.ts:1583) but the UI never sends it. So a coach who creates 'Resting HR' as Text by mistake has no in-product way to fix the type; the only path is Archive (handleArchive, line 239-259) and recreate. Archiving + recreating produces a NEW definition id, so all historical values stored under the old id in training_log_sessions.custom_metrics become orphaned: they remain in the JSON but are never rendered (the old def is excluded by fetchMetricDefinitions' archived filter, trainingLogService.ts:1551, so it never appears in any week table), and the new def starts empty.
findings.userImpact: A coach who mistypes a metric's type loses all previously-collected values for that metric the moment they correct it, because correction = archive + recreate under a fresh id. Silent historical-data loss with no warning.
findings.recommendation: Either allow editing value_type when no values have yet been recorded under the definition, or surface a clear warning on archive that values under that definition will no longer be visible. Long-term, prefer keeping the definition id stable and migrating the stored entries when a type genuinely must change. Respects 'never delete data without instruction'.

verdicts.reasoning: Every cited fact checks out in the real code. (1) The create form offers number|text: WeekMetricsSettings.tsx:415-422 renders a <select value={newType}> with Number/Text options, passed to createMetricDefinition as valueType:newType (line 188). (2) The edit form omits value_type: handleEditSave (lines 217-237) calls updateMetricDefinition(editingId, { label, unit }) at lines 223-226 — only label and unit; the inline edit UI (lines 341-369) renders only editLabel and editUnit inputs, with NO type selector anywhere. (3) updateMetricDefinition's signature DOES accept value_type (trainingLogService.ts:1583, Partial<Pick<..., 'label'|'value_type'|'unit'>>) but the UI never sends it — confirming the "type accepts it, UI never uses it" claim. (4) Correction therefore requires Archive (handleArchive, lines 239-259 → archiveMetricDefinition line 1595, sets archived_at only) + recreate (createMetricDefinition line 1557 does a fresh insert, so Postgres generates a NEW id). (5) fetchMetricDefinitions excludes archived by default (line 1551, `if (!opts.includeArchived) q = q.is('archived_at', null)`), and all three render call sites (fetchAthleteDay line 253, WeekMetricsSettings line 121, LogModeView line 87) call it WITHOUT includeArchived, so the archived def vanishes from every week table. (6) Historical values are keyed by definition id in the session custom_metrics JSON (setSessionCustomMetric line 1674-1675 writes next[definitionId]; LogWeekOverview.tsx:516 reads custom_metrics?.[def.id]), with no FK (corroborated by data.md DC-04). So once the old def is archived, its accumulated JSON values remain stored but are never rendered (the def is filtered out of customDefs), and the new def's fresh id starts empty. The bug is fully reachable: any coach who picks the wrong type at creation has no in-product edit path to fix it and must archive+recreate, losing visibility of all prior values. One precision: the data is not destroyed — it persists in the custom_metrics JSON — so this is silent data INVISIBILITY/inaccessibility rather than deletion; the archive confirm text (line 240) even claims "Past data is preserved," which is misleading in this correction scenario. That nuance doesn't lower the rating: functionally the coach loses access to historical values when correcting a mistype, with no warning, which is a genuine medium data-loss/UX issue. Severity medium upheld.

findings.title: setSessionCustomMetric is a non-atomic read-modify-write of the whole custom_metrics JSON
findings.evidence: setSessionCustomMetric does SELECT custom_metrics → mutate one key in JS → UPDATE the entire custom_metrics object (trainingLogService.ts:1665-1681). It is fired per-field from handlePatchCustomMetric (TodayScreen.tsx:405-411) inside runSave. If two custom-metric commits race on the same session (e.g. athlete blurs two fields in quick succession, or athlete + coach edit overlap), the second read can predate the first write and clobber the first key when it writes the whole map back. CLAUDE.md's last-write-wins invariant (#4) is defined per-record, not per-JSON-key, so this read-modify-write of one shared column violates the spirit of it for the sub-keys. The built-in metrics (raw_*, bodyweight_kg, vas_score) are separate columns and so are NOT exposed to this hazard — only custom metrics sharing one jsonb column are.
findings.userImpact: Two near-simultaneous custom-metric entries on one session can silently drop one of the values. Reachable with several enabled custom metrics and fast blur-to-blur entry on mobile.
findings.recommendation: Use a single atomic Postgres jsonb update (`custom_metrics = custom_metrics || jsonb_build_object(:id, :val)` for set, `custom_metrics - :id` for clear) via an RPC or migration-backed function, eliminating the read-modify-write window. Migrations stay gated per CLAUDE.md.

verdicts.reasoning:
Mechanism confirmed verbatim. setSessionCustomMetric (src/lib/trainingLogService.ts:1665-1681) is an unconditional client-side read-modify-write: SELECT custom_metrics (1665-1669) → spread+mutate one key in JS (1673-1675) → UPDATE the whole custom_metrics object back (1676-1681). There is no atomic jsonb `||`/`-` operator, no optimistic-concurrency guard, and no version/where check on the column. So if two writes interleave, the second SELECT can predate the first UPDATE and write back a map missing the first key, silently dropping it.

Reachability confirmed at three layers:
1) runSave (src/athlete/v2/screens/TodayScreen.tsx:278-301) does NOT serialize concurrent saves — it only flips a `saving` boolean; it has no queue/mutex, so two handlePatchCustomMetric calls (405-411) launch independent overlapping async chains.
2) The custom-metric inputs are NOT disabled while saving: SessionHeader.tsx:138-145 renders CustomMetricField without passing `saving`, and CustomMetricField.tsx has no `disabled` prop at all. (Contrast: line 982 passes `saving` to SessionHeader for the Saving… indicator only, and other set inputs at 904/1044/1072 ARE disabled={saving} — the custom-metric fields are not.)
3) CustomMetricField commits on blur (CustomMetricField.tsx:57) and on Enter (58). Recommendation (atomic jsonb update via gated RPC) is sound. findings.title: RAW readiness model (pillars, 1–3 scale, bands) is fully hardcoded — no coach-flexibility
findings.evidence: The four RAW pillars (Sleep/Physical/Mood/Nutrition), their 1–3 rating descriptions, and the three adjustment bands (4–6 / 7–9 / 10–12 with volume-reduction prescriptions) are compile-time constants in ELEIKO_RAW_AXES (trainingLogModel.ts:139-176) and ELEIKO_RAW_BANDS (199-230). The coach's only control is a single on/off toggle 'RAW readiness (1–3 scale)' (WeekMetricsSettings.tsx:298-303). The score-to-colour mapping is also hardcoded to a 1/2/3 scale in RawScoreDial.tsx:25-29. A coach who uses a different readiness instrument (e.g. a 1–5 Hooper index, or different pillars) cannot redefine pillars, scale, or band thresholds — only enable/disable the Eleiko one. This is the kind of OWL/monitoring concept CLAUDE.md principle #1 says must be runtime-configurable.
findings.userImpact: Coaches who do not use the Eleiko RAW model have no readiness metric they can shape; they must abuse custom metrics (which lack the dial UI, bands and guidance) instead.
findings.recommendation: Acceptable to keep Eleiko as a built-in named standard, but document it as a deliberate fixed standard (it partly is, via the comment at trainingLogModel.ts:113-124). If broader readiness flexibility is wanted, the existing custom-metric-definition machinery could be extended to a 'scale' type with coach-defined min/max and band thresholds. Low priority; flag, do not force.

verdicts.reasoning: Every cited fact checks out against the real code. ELEIKO_RAW_AXES (trainingLogModel.ts:139-176) is a compile-time const defining the four fixed pillars (sleep/physical/mood/nutrition) and their 1-3 rating descriptions; ELEIKO_RAW_BANDS (199-230) hardcodes the three bands (10-12/7-9/4-6) with their volume-reduction bullets; getRawGuidance/rawAxisRange (232-239) derive purely from those consts. RawScoreDial.tsx:25-29 hardcodes SCORE_COLOR to a 1/2/3 scale (1→red, 2→amber, 3→emerald) and renders the grid by iterating ELEIKO_RAW_AXES — so the dial is structurally locked to four pillars × 3 scores. WeekMetricsSettings.tsx:298-303 confirms the coach's only RAW control is a single ToggleRow ('RAW readiness (1–3 scale)' → toggleBuiltin('trackRaw')); there is no UI to redefine pillars, scale, or thresholds. I also verified the userImpact claim that custom metrics are not a substitute: value_type is strictly 'number' | 'text' (database.types.ts:405, trainingLogService.ts:1561) and the add-metric form offers only Number/Text (WeekMetricsSettings.tsx:415-422), so a coach cannot author a banded readiness instrument with dial/guidance — they would indeed have to abuse a plain number/text metric. This is a real, reachable architecture/flexibility gap against CLAUDE.md principle #1. Severity 'low' is correct and honest: the code explicitly documents Eleiko RAW as a deliberate named standard (trainingLogModel.ts:113-124, 'verbatim from the Eleiko reference card'), and the finding itself recommends 'flag, do not force' — it is a configurability limitation, not a runtime bug.
verdicts.correctedEvidence: All cited file:line ranges are accurate. Minor cosmetic note only: the evidence lists the bands low-to-high (4–6 / 7–9 / 10–12) while ELEIKO_RAW_BANDS is declared high-to-low (10–12 first); content is identical and the discrepancy does not affect the finding.

findings.title: VAS and custom metrics are absent from the per-day glance (LogDayCard header) and SessionPreview, unlike BW/RAW
findings.evidence: The LogDayCard header chip row surfaces Bodyweight and RAW/12 (LogDayCard.tsx:126-131) but has no branch for vas_score or custom_metrics. VAS and custom metrics appear ONLY in the LogWeekOverview OtherMetricsTable (LogWeekOverview.tsx:504-531). So a coach scanning a single day's collapsed card sees BW + RAW but never VAS or custom values for that day; they must scroll to the week table and read across columns. The athlete-side SessionPreview chip row similarly shows only BW/RAW/duration (per the shared map), so VAS/custom have no compact summary on either glance surface.
findings.userImpact: Inconsistent density: a coach reviewing one day cannot see the athlete's pain score or custom metrics at a glance, breaking the scan-friendly hierarchy EMOS aims for and making per-day VAS review require horizontal table reading.
findings.recommendation: Add a VAS chip (and optionally a compact custom-metric chip) to the LogDayCard header when those metrics are tracked and present, mirroring the existing BW/RAW chips, so per-day review is symmetric. Use a title tooltip for terse labels per CLAUDE.md chip guidance.

verdicts.reasoning: I read both cited files and the surrounding code. The asymmetry is real and reachable. LogDayCard.tsx:124-140 renders a session chip row that only branches on session.bodyweight_kg (BW, lines 126-128), session.raw_total (RAW/12, lines 129-131), and session.duration_minutes (lines 133-135). A grep confirmed LogDayCard.tsx contains NO reference to vas_score or custom_metrics anywhere. VAS and custom metrics surface ONLY in LogWeekOverview's OtherMetricsTable (lines 480-531: VAS at 504-512, custom at 513-531), which is a week-spanning table (columns: DayColumn[] across the week), so a coach must read horizontally to see a single day's VAS/custom value — there is no per-day glance for them. This data is real, coach-configurable, and athlete-entered: trackVas comes from metricsConfig.track_vas (LogWeekOverview.tsx:188) and custom_metrics from AthleteMetricDefinition entries (athlete side captures them via SessionHeader/CustomMetricField). The athlete-side SessionPreview.tsx:88-100 mirrors the same omission — its session chip row shows only BW (90-92), RAW (93-95), and duration (96-98), with no VAS/custom chip. One evidence nit: the finding says SessionPreview shows BW/RAW/duration "per the shared map," but there is no shared chip map — the two components hand-code the same chip set independently. The substance is unaffected: both glance surfaces omit VAS/custom while showing BW/RAW. This is correctly a low-severity consistency/density gap, not a functional bug — the data is fully accessible via the week table, so nothing is lost; per-day scanning is just less symmetric than CLAUDE.md's scan-friendly-hierarchy goal would prefer.
verdicts.correctedEvidence: LogDayCard.tsx:124-140 (chip row: BW 126-128, RAW 129-131, duration 133-135; no vas_score/custom_metrics). LogWeekOverview.tsx:504-531 (VAS row 504-512, custom rows 513-531, inside the week-spanning OtherMetricsTable). SessionPreview.tsx:88-100 omits VAS/custom too — but note the chips are hand-coded inline per component, NOT driven by a shared map as the original evidence stated.

findings.title: Bodyweight silently reverts garbage / 0 / negative entries with no feedback
findings.evidence: BodyweightField.commit accepts a value only when `Number.isFinite(parsed) && parsed > 0` (BodyweightField.tsx:24); otherwise it silently resets the input text back to the prior value (line 27) with no message. So an athlete who fat-fingers '8O' (letter O), types '0', or a negative, sees their entry vanish on blur with no explanation. This is correct data hygiene (no SET is created, no bad value stored) but the silent revert gives no signal that the input was rejected.
findings.userImpact: An athlete who mistypes bodyweight sees the field blank/revert on blur and may not realise their entry was discarded, leading to a missing bodyweight log.
findings.recommendation: On reject, briefly flag the field (e.g. red border + 'Enter a weight above 0') rather than silently reverting, so the athlete knows to re-enter. Minor polish; data integrity is already sound.

verdicts.reasoning:
The core mechanism is confirmed and reachable. BodyweightField.tsx:24 gates acceptance on `Number.isFinite(parsed) && parsed > 0`; the else branch at line 27 silently resets the input text back to the prior value with no error message, border flag, or toast. The field is wired into SessionHeader.tsx:127 (`onChange={onPatchBodyweight}`), which only fires on a valid parse, so a rejected entry never propagates and the athlete gets no feedback. The render path (gated by `showBw`, line 126) is genuinely reachable, and neither BodyweightField nor SessionHeader renders any rejection feedback. So the described UX problem (silent revert, no signal) is real for `'0'`, negative values, and inputs that don't begin with a digit (e.g. `'O8'`, `'abc'`).

However, the headline reproduction example is factually wrong: I verified with node that `parseFloat('8O'.replace(',','.'))` returns `8`, and `Number.isFinite(8) && 8 > 0` is true — so '8O' (digit-then-letter) is SILENTLY ACCEPTED as 8 kg, NOT reverted. parseFloat reads the leading numeric portion and discards trailing garbage. This means '8O' is arguably a worse, separate hazard (bad value silently accepted), but it is not an instance of the silent-revert behavior the finding describes. The valid trigger conditions are '0', negatives, and non-leading-digit text.

Severity stays low: data integrity is intact (no SET created, no bad value for the truly-rejected cases), and it is genuine but minor UX polish. Marking partial because the cited reproduction ('8O') does not actually trigger the described path.
verdicts.correctedEvidence: BodyweightField.tsx:24,27 — silent revert is real but triggers for `'0'`, negative values, and inputs not starting with a digit (`'O8'`, `'abc'`). The finding's `'8O'` example is incorrect: parseFloat('8O') === 8, so it is silently ACCEPTED as 8 kg (a distinct issue), not reverted.

findings.title: Numeric metric review uses period decimals (toFixed), not the EU comma convention used elsewhere
findings.evidence: OtherMetricsTable formats numeric cells and the Avg column with `Number.isInteger(v) ? v : v.toFixed(1)` (LogWeekOverview.tsx:584-592), and RawTable's Avg uses `a.toFixed(1)` (line 439) — both yield a period decimal ('77.5'). CLAUDE.md mandates comma decimals for user-facing numeric formatting (German locale), and the athlete-side inputs explicitly normalise comma↔dot (BodyweightField.tsx:21-22, CustomMetricField.tsx:30, parseNumericInput in trainingLogModel.ts:251). So input accepts commas but the coach review renders dots — an internal inconsistency with the documented convention.
findings.userImpact: Coach sees '77.5 kg' where the rest of the app and the athlete's own entry convention is comma-decimal; cosmetic locale inconsistency.
findings.recommendation: Route these displays through the shared numeric formatter used elsewhere (comma decimals) so review matches the project's stated EU convention. Trivial.

verdicts.reasoning:
The two cited code paths are confirmed verbatim and reachable: OtherMetricsTable renders numeric metric cells and the Avg column with `Number.isInteger(v) ? v : v.toFixed(1)` (LogWeekOverview.tsx:584-592) and RawTable's Avg uses `a.toFixed(1)` (line 439). Both yield period decimals (e.g. "77.5"). These are reachable whenever a coach reviews bodyweight/VAS/custom-number metrics or the RAW table — normal coach-review flow. CLAUDE.md does mandate comma decimals for user-facing numeric formatting, so a documented-convention gap genuinely exists.

However, the finding's framing as a localized "internal inconsistency" between these lines and "the rest of the app / the athlete's own convention" is inaccurate, which is why this is partial rather than confirmed:

1. The athlete-side comma handling (BodyweightField.tsx:22, CustomMetricField.tsx:30, parseNumericInput in trainingLogModel.ts:251) is INPUT-ACCEPTANCE ONLY — `text.replace(',', '.')` then parseFloat. Nothing stores or renders commas. Critically, the athlete app DISPLAYS its own bodyweight back with `value.toFixed(1)` (BodyweightField.tsx:10,13,27) — a DOT decimal. So the claim that "the athlete's own entry convention is comma-decimal" display-wise is false; the athlete sees dots too.

2. Dot-decimal display via `.toFixed()` is the DOMINANT pattern across the entire log/planner surface, including LogWeekOverview itself: performed/planned kg averages `${performedAvg.toFixed(1)} kg` (lines 221-222), tonnage `(n/1000).toFixed(1)` (line 44), RAW total `avg(ts)!.toFixed(1)` (line 460), plus PlanActual.tsx:32, LogDayCard.tsx:127, PrintWeek.tsx:512, WeekSummaryBox.tsx:93. The two cited lines are therefore CONSISTENT with their own surface, not anomalous within it.

3. The only comma-decimal display in the codebase is the separate Analysis module (analysis/builder/format.ts:6 and PivotTable.tsx:32,224 via `toLocaleString('de-DE')`). So the true gap is project-wide (the whole log/planner layer ignores the comma mandate while Analysis honours it), not a localized inconsistency between these lines and a comma-using neighbour.

Net: a real cosmetic deviation from the documented EU convention exists at the cited lines, but it is part of a much broader, uniform dot-decimal display pattern rather than the localized internal inconsistency the finding describes, and the supporting claim about the athlete display convention is wrong. Severity correctly stays at nit (purely cosmetic; no functional impact, numbers parse and round-trip correctly).
verdicts.correctedEvidence: LogWeekOverview.tsx:584-592 and :439 do render period decimals against CLAUDE.md's comma mandate, but this is not a localized inconsistency — the same dot-decimal display is used throughout the log/planner layer (LogWeekOverview.tsx:44,221-222,460; PlanActual.tsx:32; LogDayCard.tsx:127; PrintWeek.tsx:512; WeekSummaryBox.tsx:93). The athlete app also displays dots (BodyweightField.tsx:10,13,27); its comma handling is input-only. Comma-decimal display exists only in the separate Analysis module (analysis/builder/format.ts:6, PivotTable.tsx:32). The accurate framing is a project-wide convention gap, not a discrepancy between these two lines and the rest of the app.

findings.title: Custom metric definitions are strictly per-athlete with no group/template concept
findings.evidence: createMetricDefinition is keyed to a single athleteId (trainingLogService.ts:1557-1566) and WeekMetricsSettings is opened per athlete+week. There is no group-level or template definition; the only seeding helper auto-enables the new def for the current week of that one athlete (WeekMetricsSettings.tsx:191-200). A coach who wants the same custom metric (e.g. 'Lower back pain') across a 12-athlete group must recreate it 12 times, and each recreation is an independent id (so cross-athlete aggregation by 'the same metric' is not possible).
findings.userImpact: For a squad, configuring a common custom metric is N manual creations with no shared identity, raising interaction cost (counter to EMOS's low-interaction-cost goal) and preventing group-level comparison of the same metric.
findings.recommendation: Consider a coach-level metric template that can be applied to athletes/groups (re-using the existing definition shape), or at least a 'copy metric to other athletes' action. Out of immediate scope but worth a backlog note; do not over-build.

verdicts.reasoning: Every structural claim checks out against the real code and schema. (1) createMetricDefinition (trainingLogService.ts:1557-1578) inserts a row with only athlete_id, owner_id, label, value_type, unit — no group_id or template_id. (2) The DB migration 20260519000002_add_metric_toggles.sql:34-44 defines athlete_metric_definitions with `athlete_id uuid not null references athletes(id)` and id `default gen_random_uuid()`, and has NO group/template column; the AthleteMetricDefinition type (database.types.ts:400-410) matches exactly. (3) WeekMetricsSettings is invoked per athlete+week in LogModeView.tsx:239-243 (athleteId + weekStart props). (4) handleAdd (WeekMetricsSettings.tsx:184-200) creates the def for the single athleteId and auto-enables it only for that athlete's current week — no propagation. (5) A repo-wide grep for any group/template/copy metric helper (metric.*group|copyMetric|applyMetric|metric.*template|seedMetric) found zero matches in src/; the only "group" hits are the unrelated Analysis module's group dimension/plans. (6) custom_metrics on sessions is keyed by athlete_metric_definitions.id (migration:22-29), and each new def gets an independent uuid, so two athletes' identically-named metrics are distinct ids — cross-athlete aggregation "by the same metric" is genuinely impossible. So a coach configuring a common metric across an N-athlete squad must recreate it N times. This is a real, reachable design limitation rather than a defect in existing behavior, and the reviewer itself frames it as a backlog/scalability note ("out of immediate scope... do not over-build"). Low severity is correct: it is a missing-feature/interaction-cost concern (counter to EMOS's low-interaction-cost goal), not a runtime bug.

findings.title: Three parallel 'metrics' vocabularies coexist; Analysis metrics persist only to localStorage
findings.evidence: Three unrelated metric systems share the word 'metrics': (a) session wellbeing metrics (this review's core, DB-backed), (b) the Analysis aggregation registry (metricRegistry.ts), and (c) the legacy planner-summary metrics.ts (reps/sets/max/avg/tonnage/k). metricRegistry.ts:6 explicitly states it 'is intended to subsume src/lib/metrics.ts' but both still ship — a known principle-#3 (single source of truth) violation in progress, and the two compute tonnage differently (metrics.ts:102 mixes % loads into avg×reps; metricRegistry.ts:99 deliberately excludes % loads), so they will disagree on the same plan. Separately, coach-defined Analysis derived metrics persist ONLY to localStorage (coachMetrics.ts:8,38-40), so they are per-browser and lost on a second device. None of these touch athlete logging — included only to disambiguate the overloaded term and confirm the user's 'Metrics function' concern lives in the session-metrics path, not here.
findings.userImpact: No athlete-facing impact. Coach-facing: Analysis-derived metrics silently vanish across devices/browsers (localStorage), and the two surviving tonnage definitions can report different numbers for the same plan, eroding trust in Analysis figures.
findings.recommendation: Outside the athlete training-log lane, but track: (1) complete the metrics.ts → metricRegistry.ts consolidation so tonnage has one definition; (2) move coachMetrics off localStorage onto the gated analysis_metrics table (DC-02) when that ships. No action needed within the Metrics-translation scope itself.

verdicts.reasoning: Every cited claim checks out against the real code. (1) Three "metrics" vocabularies genuinely coexist: session wellbeing metrics (not these files), the Analysis registry (metricRegistry.ts), and the legacy planner-summary metrics.ts. Both metricRegistry.ts and metrics.ts still ship — metrics.ts is actively imported by 9+ live planner files (DayCard.tsx:15, WeeklyPlanner.tsx:17, PlannerControlPanel.tsx:18, WeekSummaryBox.tsx:17, GeneralSettings.tsx:7, usePlannerWeekOverview.ts:4, MetricStrip.tsx:1, etc.), while metricRegistry.ts powers the Analysis engine. The "intended to subsume src/lib/metrics.ts" comment is verbatim at metricRegistry.ts:6, confirming the in-progress principle-#3 violation. (2) The tonnage-divergence claim is real and reachable: metrics.ts:102 does `tonnage += avg * r` using `summary_avg_load` unconditionally (no kg gate), so a percentage-unit exercise whose summary_avg_load holds a % value is folded into the kg sum; metricRegistry.ts:99 gates the same metric behind `kg(r)` (metricRegistry.ts:37-39 = `row.loadIsKg && row.load > 0`), deliberately excluding % loads. For any plan with percentage-unit counting exercises the two will report different tonnage. (3) coachMetrics persist ONLY to localStorage (coachMetrics.ts:8 KEY, :38-40 saveCoachMetricSpecs) and are wired into the live registry via AnalysisModule.tsx:51/72/75 (createRegistry(coachSpecs.map(specToMetric))), so they are per-browser and lost on a second device — confirmed reachable, not theoretical. (4) The finding's self-scoping is accurate: a grep of src/athlete found zero references to computeMetrics, lib/metrics, metricRegistry, or coachMetrics, so none of these touch athlete logging. Severity low is correct — no athlete-facing impact, and the coach-facing impacts (divergent tonnage, per-browser metric loss) are genuine but secondary architecture/trust concerns explicitly outside the athlete-training-log review lane. The finding is honest about being informational/disambiguating.

findings.dimensionOverview: The session-metrics subsystem (RAW / Bodyweight / VAS / custom number|text) is the cleanest part of the training-log: every metric writes session-level columns/JSON and NONE generates a training_log_set, so the user's "free text creates a set" complaint does not live here — that bug is in the planned-exercise prescription path, not Metrics. The round-trip is mostly correct: coach toggles in WeekMetricsSettings → athlete renders the matching control in SessionHeader → values save to raw_*/bodyweight_kg/vas_score/custom_metrics → coach reviews in LogWeekOverview tables. enabledMetricDefs is filtered identically on both athlete (TodayScreen.tsx:970-976) and coach (LogModeView.tsx:278-282) sides, so an archived definition cleanly disappears from both surfaces, and BW=null vs 0 is distinguishable in the coach table. That said, there are real defects: (1) the DB column defaults (track_raw/bw DEFAULT false) directly contradict the app's no-config fallback (RAW+BW on), a latent single-source-of-truth landmine that flips to coach-toggled-off truth the day a row is ever inserted by anything other than the popover; (2) VAS is documented and stored as supporting 0.5-step / 1-decimal precision but the athlete slider hardcodes step=1, so the coach-promised granularity is unreachable, and a null VAS is visually indistinguishable from a logged 0 because the thumb defaults to 0; (3) text custom metrics are hard-truncated to 14 chars in the only coach review surface, making a "Pain area" note unreadable; (4) value_type cannot be edited after creation so a mistyped number/text metric can only be archived+recreated, orphaning historical values; (5) setSessionCustomMetric is a non-atomic read-modify-write of the whole custom_metrics JSON; (6) the RAW Eleiko 4-pillar scale, bands and labels are fully hardcoded — a coach cannot define an alternative readiness model, a coach-flexibility (principle #1) tension, though it is an intentional named-standard. The Analysis-module "metrics" (metricRegistry/coachMetrics) are a separate vocabulary that never touches athlete logging; I flag the three-vocabulary duplication and localStorage-only persistence only for disambiguation.

findings.title: free_text_reps with no parsable "× reps" produces a dead, un-loggable card — athlete cannot mark it done or skipped
findings.evidence: When unit==='free_text_reps' and planned.setLines is empty, ExerciseLogCard.tsx:109 re-parses prescription_raw via parseFreeTextPrescription. parseFreeTextSegment (prescriptionParser.ts:190-210) returns null unless the segment splits on x/× into exactly 2 or 3 numeric parts. So a coach who types prose with no separator (e.g. "Technique work" or "Easy snatch pulls") yields lines=[] → base=[] → rows=[] (line 139). The render then hits ExerciseLogCard.tsx:332-335 and shows 'No set lines defined for this exercise' with NO ✓/✗, NO Add-set inside the empty branch, and NO Mark-complete. detectIntendedUnit forces free_text_reps on ANY letter in a load cell, so this input is trivially reachable from normal coach authoring.
findings.userImpact: The athlete sees a prescribed exercise they physically cannot acknowledge — no Done, no Skip, no note row. The session can never reach 'all complete' and the coach sees it as perpetually un-logged. This is exactly the 'free text is buggy' behaviour the user flagged, taken to its worst end.
findings.recommendation: Route the empty-parse free_text_reps case down the same single merged ✓/✗ row path that free_text/'other' already use (ExerciseLogCard.tsx:125-135): if free_text_reps yields zero parsed rows, synthesise one freeTextMode row carrying prescription_raw. This makes every prose prescription acknowledgeable without generating a numeric set, matching the user's stated intent.

verdicts.reasoning:
Re-read all three cited files plus the coach write path and the unit-detection/persistence logic. The bug is real and reachable end-to-end.

REACHABILITY OF THE INPUT (this is the crux). The finding attributed reachability to grid authoring + detectIntendedUnit, but the PrescriptionGrid for free_text_reps always renders a numeric reps cell and saves via formatFreeTextPrescription (prescriptionParser.ts:332-344) which always emits "loadText × reps" — so the grid alone CANNOT produce a separator-less prescription. However, there is a cleaner, definitely-reachable path the finding missed: ExerciseDetail.tsx has a "Text mode" toggle (button at :562-569) exposing a raw textarea (:573-577) whose Apply handler applyText() (:212-222) runs detectIntendedUnit(textValue) and persists. detectIntendedUnit (prescriptionParser.ts:26-37) returns 'free_text_reps' for ANY input containing a letter after stripping x/X/×/* — so prose like "Technique work" → unit='free_text_reps', prescription_raw="Technique work".

PERSISTENCE GUARANTEES EMPTY setLines. In useWeekPlans.writePrescription, hasNumericLines = parsed.length>0 && !isNonNumeric && !isFreeTextReps (useWeekPlans.ts:530). Because isFreeTextReps is true, hasNumericLines is ALWAYS false for free_text_reps → lines=[] → replaceSetLines(id, []) (:541-542). So EVERY free_text_reps exercise stores zero planned_set_lines; the athlete card therefore always takes the fallback branch at ExerciseLogCard.tsx:109.

DEAD CARD CONFIRMED. parseFreeTextPrescription("Technique work") → single segment, no x/× → parts.length===1 in parseFreeTextSegment (prescriptionParser.ts:190-210) → neither the ===2 nor ===3 branch hits → returns null → result=[] → base=[] → rows=[] (ExerciseLogCard.tsx:139). Render hits :332-335 showing "No set lines defined for this exercise". The "Add set" button (:476-484) and Mark-complete (:349-357) are both INSIDE the rows.length>0 else-branch (:336), so the empty branch offers NO ✓/✗, NO Add-set, NO Mark-complete. The athlete cannot acknowledge or skip; completedCount/rows.length is 0/— so allCompleted is false forever and the session never reaches all-complete. This is exactly the user's "free text is buggy" concern taken to its worst end, and the recommended fix (synthesise a single freeTextMode row like the free_text/'other' path at :125-135) is consistent with existing code.

SEVERITY: Adjusted critical→high. It is a genuine reachable dead-end with no athlete workaround for that exercise (correctly high), but it does not corrupt data, block the rest of the session's exercises, or crash; it requires the coach to deliberately use Text mode with separator-less prose. High, not critical.
verdicts.correctedEvidence: Primary reachable input path is ExerciseDetail.tsx:212-222 (applyText) via the "Text mode" toggle at ExerciseDetail.tsx:562-569 / textarea :573-577, NOT the grid (the grid always emits "× reps"). Empty setLines for free_text_reps is GUARANTEED by useWeekPlans.ts:530 (hasNumericLines excludes isFreeTextReps → replaceSetLines with []). Dead-render and missing controls confirmed at ExerciseLogCard.tsx:332-335 (empty branch) vs :336/:349-357/:476-484 (Add-set + Mark-complete only in the non-empty branch); parse-returns-null confirmed at prescriptionParser.ts:190-210; detectIntendedUnit forcing free_text_reps on any letter confirmed at prescriptionParser.ts:26-37.

verdicts.reasoning:
The bug is real and reachable. Verified the full chain:

1. free_text_reps NEVER persists planned_set_lines. useWeekPlans.ts:530 sets `hasNumericLines = parsed.length > 0 && !isNonNumeric && !isFreeTextReps`, so `lines = []` is always written for free_text_reps (line 531-542). The athlete card therefore ALWAYS takes the parseFreeTextPrescription fallback (ExerciseLogCard.tsx:109), never expandSetLines.

2. parseFreeTextSegment (prescriptionParser.ts:190-209) returns null unless a segment splits on x/× into 2-3 parts with a numeric reps. Bare prose ("Technique work") → parts.length===1 → null → lines=[] → base=[] → rows=[] (ExerciseLogCard.tsx:139).

3. With rows.length===0 the render hits the else-less branch at ExerciseLogCard.tsx:332-335 ("No set lines defined…"). The entire interactive block (lines 337-486: Log-as-prescribed, Mark-complete, the ✓/✗ SetEntryRows, Add-set) is the OTHER arm of the ternary and is NOT rendered. Only the notes textarea (488-503) survives. So no Done, no Skip, no Mark-complete — confirmed.

4. The exercise can never auto-complete: expectedPlannedSetCount returns null for empty-parse free_text_reps (plannedSetCount.ts:23), and isExerciseDone returns false when le.sets.length===0 (trainingLogModel.ts:86). The card stays pending forever; the coach sees it perpetually un-logged. Matches the stated userImpact.

REACHABILITY (the original citation understated this — correcting): the structured PrescriptionGrid CANNOT produce the dead card, because formatFreeTextPrescription always appends "× {reps}" (prescriptionParser.ts:338,340) and grid reps is a clamped numeric column. The actual reachable path is the free-form Text mode in ExerciseDetail.tsx: applyText() (lines 214-219) saves the textarea verbatim as prescription_raw with unit = detectIntendedUnit(textValue); any letter → free_text_reps (prescriptionParser.ts:29). A coach typing "Technique work" → unit=free_text_reps, prescription_raw="Technique work" (no ×) → dead card. This is normal authoring, so the bug is genuinely reachable, not theoretical.

FIX SOUNDNESS: The recommendation (synthesize one freeTextMode ✓/✗ row when free_text_reps yields zero parsed rows, mirroring the free_text/other branch at ExerciseLogCard.tsx:125-135) is correct in direction and matches the user's intent ("just accepted, or marked did not do — should NOT generate a set") and the planned-vs-logged separation (load saves null; no numeric set fabricated). One implementation caveat the implementer must handle: isFreeTextUnit (line 157) is only `free_text || other`, so several render guards (lines 338, 435, 464) won't treat free_text_reps as free-text; the synthesized row's own `freeTextMode:true` drives SetEntryRow correctly, but isFreeTextUnit/extra-row logic should be broadened or the fix scoped so it doesn't accidentally re-enable Log-as-prescribed for the prose case. It does not break the normal (with-×) free_text_reps path, which still parses to real rows. So the fix is sound with care.

SEVERITY: Adjusting critical → high. The defect is real and user-facing (an un-acknowledgeable prescribed exercise that keeps a session perpetually incomplete), but there is no data loss/corruption, the notes textarea still gives the athlete a communication channel, and a coach workaround exists (use the grid, or append "× N"). That is a serious robustness bug worth fixing, but not catastrophic — "high" is the honest level.
verdicts.correctedEvidence: Dead-card condition confirmed at src/athlete/v2/components/ExerciseLogCard.tsx:109-124 (empty parse → base=[]) and 332-335 (only the no-controls branch renders). Root cause that free_text_reps never stores set lines: src/hooks/useWeekPlans.ts:530-542. Empty-parse predicate: src/lib/prescriptionParser.ts:190-209. Cannot auto-complete: src/lib/plannedSetCount.ts:21-25 + src/lib/trainingLogModel.ts:86. REACHABILITY (add to citation): the bare-prose free_text_reps raw is created via the free-form Text mode in src/components/planner/ExerciseDetail.tsx:214-219 (applyText saves textValue verbatim with unit from detectIntendedUnit), since the PrescriptionGrid path always appends "× reps" (src/lib/prescriptionParser.ts:338,340) and so cannot trigger it.

findings.dimensionOverview: The two surfaces are well-built in many respects: the athlete dark theme is internally consistent, set-entry has good optimistic-save discipline, destructive single-set deletes route through an UndoToast and bulk/irreversible deletes through a ConfirmModal (TodayScreen.tsx), and the coach Log review reuses the canonical StackedNotation so plan/did read coherently. The shared StackedNotation/SentinelDisplay and the GroupLogView (LogModeView surfaces) correctly use design tokens. However there are several reachable UX defects that violate stated EMOS rules. (1) The user's headline concern is real and visually under-served: free_text_reps produces kg-labelled numeric SetEntryRows (load cell unit="kg") even though the planned "load" is prose, and a free_text_reps prescription with no parsable "× reps" yields a dead "No set lines defined" card with no way to accept/skip — the athlete cannot log it. (2) The set-vs-accept distinction is NOT visually signalled: a free_text exercise renders the same ✓/✗ pill chrome and the same card as a quantified set, so the athlete/coach can't tell "this is an accept-only note" from "this is a real set" at a glance. (3) European date conventions (a non-negotiable CLAUDE.md rule) are violated in EVERY date render on both surfaces — SessionHeader, SessionPreview, WeekScreen, WeekNavigator, LogDayCard and logFormatUtils all call toLocaleDateString with US-style month-first options or h:mm AM/PM, bypassing the existing DD/MM helpers in dateUtils.ts. (4) The entire coach Log subsystem is hand-rolled with raw gray-*/blue-*/emerald-* Tailwind literals and bg-blue-600 buttons instead of the Button primitive and design tokens, which the design-system section forbids on coach surfaces. (5) Percentage/RPE prescriptions mislabel the athlete entry cell as "kg" and a one-tap ✓ back-fills the % / RPE number as a kg performed_load — a silent mislog the coach then reviews as truth. (6) Several tap targets fall below the 40px mobile guideline (RAW score buttons h-8=32px, GPP done checkbox 24px, icon buttons p-1≈28px). (7) The "RAW n/12" chip and "Combo" chip render on every applicable row, carrying no signal per the CLAUDE.md chip rule. (8) Custom text metrics are hard-truncated to 14 chars in the only coach review surface. Findings below are evidence-cited and ordered by severity.

findings.title: free_text_reps emits kg-labelled numeric set rows for prose loads — the 'free text also creates a SET' bug the user described
findings.evidence: For free_text_reps that DOES parse (e.g. coach typed 'moderate × 5 × 3'), ExerciseLogCard.tsx:112-123 synthesises one numeric SetEntryRow per parsed line with plannedLoadText=prose ('moderate') and plannedLoadValue=null. SetEntryRow renders the standard two-cell grid (line 253) where the load NumericCell carries unit='kg' (line 256-261). So the athlete is shown a kg input box pre-hinted with the word 'moderate' and a 'kg' suffix for an exercise the coach never quantified in kg. The prose load is demoted to a greyed placeholder that vanishes the moment the athlete types.
findings.userImpact: A prose prescription is presented as a numeric kg set. The coach's intent ('moderate effort') is invisible once interacted with, and the athlete is nudged to type a kg number that was never asked for. The set-vs-note semantics the user wants are inverted: prose becomes a quantified set row.
findings.recommendation: For free_text_reps, either (a) suppress the kg load cell and render the prose load read-only beside an editable reps cell, or (b) treat it like free_text (single ✓/✗ accept row) per the user's directive. Whichever is chosen, the load cell must not be labelled 'kg' when plannedLoadValue is null — pass the unit through expandSetLines/the synthesis so the cell label reflects the actual prescription type.

verdicts.reasoning:
Re-read ExerciseLogCard.tsx:94-140, SetEntryRow.tsx:114-275, and prescriptionParser.ts:26-37,172-210. The path is reachable and the bug manifests exactly as described.

Reachability of the coach input: detectIntendedUnit (prescriptionParser.ts:29) flips the unit to free_text_reps when ANY non-separator letter appears. parseFreeTextPrescription (lines 172-210) parses a segment ONLY when it has an x/× separator and a numeric reps part, returning loadText=prose, reps, sets. So a realistic coach prescription like "moderate × 5 × 3" produces loadText="moderate", reps=5, sets=3 — exactly the cited example.

Rendering: ExerciseLogCard.tsx:109-124 takes the free_text_reps + non-empty prescription_raw branch and synthesises one SetRowInput per parsed set with plannedLoadText=prose ("moderate"), plannedLoadValue=null, and crucially does NOT set freeTextMode (contrast line 132 where the free_text/other branch DOES set freeTextMode:true). With freeTextMode falsy, SetEntryRow.tsx:252-275 renders the standard two-cell numeric grid: a load NumericCell with the hardcoded unit="kg" (line 257) and placeholder={input.plannedLoadText} = "moderate". The "kg" suffix renders unconditionally (NumericCell line 321, since unit is the non-empty string "kg"). The prose is shown only as the input placeholder (placeholder:text-gray-600, line 319) and disappears the instant the athlete types — accurate.

So the athlete is presented a kg-labelled numeric set input for an exercise the coach never quantified in kg, with the coach's prose intent demoted to a vanishing placeholder. unit="kg" is unconditional — there is no path that threads the real prescription unit through to the cell label (confirmed by grep: the only unit="kg" occurrence is the hardcoded one).

Persistence nuance: the synthesized rows are display-only until the athlete interacts; commit() (SetEntryRow.tsx:100-168) only fires on onBlur/button-click, so a DB set is written on interaction, not auto-created on render. This does not weaken the finding — the user's complaint ("free text ALSO creates a SET") is about the inverted set-vs-note semantics presented to the athlete, and the rendered quantified kg set row is exactly that manifestation. The finding's evidence, line cites (ExerciseLogCard 109-124, SetEntryRow 252-275 and 336-339), and recommendation are all grounded in the real code. Severity high is appropriate: it is the precise pattern the user flagged and degrades data quality (nudging spurious kg entry against a non-kg prescription).

The core bug is REAL and reachable. Coach side: useWeekPlans.ts:530,541 — when unit==='free_text_reps', hasNumericLines is false so NO planned_set_lines are written. Athlete side: ExerciseLogCard.tsx:107-124 — with planned.setLines empty and unit free_text_reps, it falls into the synthesis branch and pushes one row per parsed line carrying plannedLoadText=prose (e.g. 'moderate'), plannedLoadValue:null, and crucially WITHOUT setting freeTextMode. So SetEntryRow takes the numeric-grid else-branch (SetEntryRow.tsx:252-275) and renders the load NumericCell with unit="kg" (line 257) and placeholder "moderate" (line 256/317). Reproduction is concrete: coach types "moderate × 5 × 3" (parseFreeTextSegment, prescriptionParser.ts:190-210 requires an x/× separator + numeric reps) → 3 rows each showing a kg input box hinted "moderate". Confirmed.

Why PARTIAL not confirmed: severity "high" and the framing are inflated. (1) No bad data is written: in SetEntryRow.commit (lines 127-157), if the athlete only taps ✓, performedLoad = parsedLoad ?? input.plannedLoadValue = null ?? null = null, and plannedLoad is passed as null — so prose is never coerced into a fabricated kg number. The title's "prose becomes a quantified set row" overstates it; the row is mislabelled, not silently miswritten. (2) The rep/set structure (5×3) is legitimately coach-authored — synthesising rep rows is correct; only the LOAD cell is wrong. So this is a UX/data-fidelity defect (misleading 'kg' label + numeric keypad for a non-kg load, and the prose load is a vanishing placeholder never captured as performed data), not a logging/data-corruption bug. It sits in exactly the area the user flagged and is well above a nit, but a workaround exists (tap ✓). Medium is the honest severity.

Fix soundness: Option (a) — suppress the kg load cell, render the prose load read-only beside an editable reps cell — is correct and EMOS-aligned: it preserves the coach's reps/sets structure (coach-flexibility, data fidelity) and fixes the mislabel by mirroring the existing freeTextMode merged-cell pattern while keeping reps editable. Threading unit through additively (default 'kg') would NOT break absolute_kg/expandSetLines or percentage (those go through expandSetLines, which writes set lines and never hits this synthesis branch — verified at useWeekPlans.ts:530). Caveat the implementer must heed: Option (b) "treat like free_text (single ✓/✗)" is the WEAKER choice — it would discard the coach-authored 5×3 rep structure, a regression against data fidelity; prefer (a). Also note percentage/RPE rows rendered via expandSetLines are ALSO labelled 'kg' (SetEntryRow.tsx:257) — a related but separate issue out of scope for this finding.
verdicts.correctedEvidence: Load-bearing evidence: synthesis in ExerciseLogCard.tsx:109-124 (free_text_reps rows pushed without freeTextMode, plannedLoadValue:null) + rendering in SetEntryRow.tsx:252-261 (numeric grid; load NumericCell hardcoded unit="kg" at line 257). The cited SetEntryRow.tsx:336-339 is inside expandSetLines (the structured-line path) and is NOT involved in the free_text_reps synthesis bug. Root cause for "no set lines": useWeekPlans.ts:530,541.

findings.title: Percentage / RPE prescriptions mislabel the entry cell as 'kg' and one-tap ✓ silently back-fills the %/RPE number as a kg load
findings.evidence: expandSetLines (SetEntryRow.tsx:330-352) builds plannedLoadText/plannedLoadValue purely from load_value, ignoring unit. For a 'percentage' exercise load_value stores the bare percent (e.g. 80) and for an 'rpe' exercise it stores the RPE number. The load cell is hard-coded unit='kg' (line 256). Worse, on ✓ with empty cells, commit() (lines 133-141) sets performedLoad = input.plannedLoadValue — so tapping the green check on an 80% row writes performed_load=80 with status 'completed'. The card header StackedNotation DOES append '%' (StackedNotation.tsx:167) but the entry cell and the saved value do not, so header and input disagree and the stored truth is wrong.
findings.userImpact: An athlete who taps ✓ to mark a percentage/RPE set done logs '80 kg' (or 'RPE 8 kg'). The coach's Log review (LoggedStackedNotation + PlanActual 'Avg kg') then averages those numbers as kilograms (LogExerciseRow.tsx:320-321), so the coach sees a fabricated kg history. This violates EMOS principle 4 (compliance derived by comparison must be meaningful) and good-practice 'never present one unit's value under another unit's label'.
findings.recommendation: Thread unit into expandSetLines and label the entry cell '%' / 'RPE' (or render percentage read-only and require explicit kg entry). For percentage/RPE, ✓-without-value must NOT back-fill plannedLoadValue into performed_load (only absolute_kg should). The existing 'Log as prescribed' gate already does this correctly (ExerciseLogCard.tsx:238-241) — apply the same unit guard to the SetEntryRow back-fill.

verdicts.reasoning:
I traced the full data path and every link in the chain holds for the percentage case (the unambiguous, fully-reachable one):

1) Authoring/storage: parsePrescription strips "%" (prescriptionParser.ts:61) then parseSegment parses the bare number as load (parseSegment:80-95). useWeekPlans.writePrescription treats unit='percentage' as numeric (hasNumericLines is true because percentage is neither isNonNumeric nor isFreeTextReps, useWeekPlans.ts:529-530) and writes load_value: line.load (useWeekPlans.ts:537). So a prescription like "80%×3×3" persists planned_set_lines.load_value = 80 (the bare percent), with planned_exercises.unit = 'percentage'.

2) Athlete card: fetchPlannedDay returns those set lines verbatim (trainingLogService.ts:281-314). ExerciseLogCard builds rows via expandSetLines because setLines.length > 0 (ExerciseLogCard.tsx:107-108). expandSetLines ignores unit and sets plannedLoadText/plannedLoadValue purely from load_value (SetEntryRow.tsx:336-346) → plannedLoadValue = 80. Percentage is NOT a free-text unit (isFreeTextUnit only covers free_text/other, ExerciseLogCard.tsx:157), so SetEntryRow renders the NumericCell with hard-coded unit="kg" (SetEntryRow.tsx:254-257) and placeholder "80".

3) The ✓ back-fill: setDone → commit({status:'completed'}); with empty load, parsedLoad is null and completing is true, so performedLoad = input.plannedLoadValue = 80 (SetEntryRow.tsx:133-135). performed_load = 80 is persisted with status 'completed'. Confirmed.

4) Header/cell disagreement: StackedNotation appends "%" only for unit==='percentage' (StackedNotation.tsx:167-168), so the header reads "80%" while the entry cell label reads "kg" — exactly the mismatch claimed.

5) Coach review downstream: after ✓, performed_load is non-null so LoggedStackedNotation's allNonNumeric shortcut (StackedNotation.tsx:227-230) does NOT fire; it renders 80 as a kg-style column. LogExerciseRow labels the summary Avg/Max with a hard-coded unit="kg" regardless of planned unit (LogExerciseRow.tsx:320-321). So the coach sees fabricated "80 kg" history. Confirmed.

6) The recommendation's asymmetry is real: the bulk "Log as prescribed" path is correctly gated on unit==='absolute_kg' (canLogAsPrescribed, ExerciseLogCard.tsx:238-241), but the per-row ✓ back-fill applies no such unit guard.

The RPE half of the title is also plausible but slightly less airtight: the set-lines write path treats unit='rpe' as numeric too (same hasNumericLines logic), so an RPE prescription that parses as load×reps (e.g. "8×3×3") would store load_value=8 and back-fill 8 the same way — yet computePrescriptionSummary classifies 'rpe' as text-based (prescriptionParser.ts:385), an internal inconsistency, and a coach is more likely to author RPE with letters ("RPE 8") which detectIntendedUnit routes to free_text_reps. The percentage case alone is sufficient to confirm the finding at high severity: a silent unit corruption that fabricates coach-facing kg history from a single tap, violating the "never present one unit's value under another's label" rule. All cited lines are accurate.

Verified end-to-end against the actual code. For the percentage case (the dominant, standard OWL prescription type) the bug is fully reachable and silent:

1. Authoring: parsePrescription strips "%" (prescriptionParser.ts:61) and stores the bare percent in load (e.g. "80%×3×5" → {load:80}). writePrescription persists that as planned_set_lines.load_value (useWeekPlans.ts:529-541, load_value: line.load) because percentage is neither isNonNumeric nor isFreeTextReps, so hasNumericLines is true.
2. Athlete render: ExerciseLogCard.tsx:107-108 takes the expandSetLines(planned.setLines) branch first. expandSetLines (SetEntryRow.tsx:330-352) builds plannedLoadText="80"/plannedLoadValue=80 purely from load_value, ignoring unit; SetEntryRow is never passed the unit and hard-codes the load cell unit="kg" (SetEntryRow.tsx:257; finding said 256 — off by one but correct). The card header DOES append "%" (StackedNotation.tsx:167-168), so header ("80%") and entry cell ("80 kg") disagree.
3. Back-fill: commit() (SetEntryRow.tsx:133-135) on ✓ with empty cells sets performedLoad = input.plannedLoadValue = 80, status completed — no unit guard. So tapping the green check on an 80% set silently writes performed_load=80.
4. Downstream truth: coach Log review labels Avg/Max unconditionally "kg" (LogExerciseRow.tsx:320-321) computed from performed_load (logSummary.ts:53-58), and LogWeekOverview.tsx:78-79 even folds performed_load into weekly TONNAGE (performed_load × reps). So an 80% set fabricates an "80 kg" lift in the coach's history and inflates tonnage — broader impact than the finding stated.

Inconsistency is real and the recommended fix already exists in-codebase: canLogAsPrescribed correctly gates the bulk button to unit==='absolute_kg' (ExerciseLogCard.tsx:238-241); the per-row ✓ back-fill is simply missing that same guard. Threading unit and labeling the cell %/RPE is sound and consistent with EMOS principles (planned-vs-logged separation, single-source-of-truth, athlete app is intentionally dark so no token concern). The fix is safe for other types: free_text_reps already passes plannedLoadValue:null (ExerciseLogCard.tsx:120), free_text/other use freeTextMode (no numeric back-fill), and absolute_kg back-fill stays correct. NOTE the fix must also cover combos, which store the bare percent in load_value the same way (useWeekPlans.ts:520).

One scope correction on the RPE half: RPE is treated as isTextBased in both the summary helper (prescriptionParser.ts:385) and the save path, so an RPE prescription written the normal way ("RPE 8 × 3") parses to zero numeric lines (parseFloat("rpe8")=NaN, parseSegment returns null) — and ExerciseLogCard has NO rpe fallback branch (only free_text_reps/free_text/other), so that yields a separate "No set lines defined" dead-card failure, not this mislabel. The RPE mislabel described here is only reachable when the coach authors RPE as bare numbers (e.g. "8×3×5") under unit=rpe, which produces load_value=8. So the RPE claim is real but narrower than stated; the percentage claim (and combos) is the solid, common case. Severity high is justified: marking a set "done as prescribed" is the single most common athlete action, the corruption is silent, and it poisons the coach's kg history and tonnage analytics.
verdicts.correctedEvidence: Core path confirmed: load cell hard-coded unit at SetEntryRow.tsx:257 (finding said :256); back-fill at SetEntryRow.tsx:133-135; expandSetLines at :330-352. Root cause of percentage load_value is prescriptionParser.ts:61 (strips %) + useWeekPlans.ts:529-541 (load_value: line.load). Coach-side fabricated-kg surfaces: LogExerciseRow.tsx:320-321 AND additionally LogWeekOverview.tsx:78-79 (tonnage). RPE caveat: reachable only for bare-number RPE prescriptions; the common "RPE 8 …" form instead hits a separate dead-card path because ExerciseLogCard.tsx:94-135 has no rpe rows fallback.

findings.dimensionOverview: The two surfaces are well-built in many respects: the athlete dark theme is internally consistent, set-entry has good optimistic-save discipline, destructive single-set deletes route through an UndoToast and bulk/irreversible deletes through a ConfirmModal (TodayScreen.tsx), and the coach Log review reuses the canonical StackedNotation so plan/did read coherently. The shared StackedNotation/SentinelDisplay and the GroupLogView (LogModeView surfaces) correctly use design tokens. However there are several reachable UX defects that violate stated EMOS rules. (1) The user's headline concern is real and visually under-served: free_text_reps produces kg-labelled numeric SetEntryRows (load cell unit="kg") even though the planned "load" is prose, and a free_text_reps prescription with no parsable "× reps" yields a dead "No set lines defined" card with no way to accept/skip — the athlete cannot log it. (2) The set-vs-accept distinction is NOT visually signalled: a free_text exercise renders the same ✓/✗ pill chrome and the same card as a quantified set, so the athlete/coach can't tell "this is an accept-only note" from "this is a real set" at a glance. (3) European date conventions (a non-negotiable CLAUDE.md rule) are violated in EVERY date render on both surfaces — SessionHeader, SessionPreview, WeekScreen, WeekNavigator, LogDayCard and logFormatUtils all call toLocaleDateString with US-style month-first options or h:mm AM/PM, bypassing the existing DD/MM helpers in dateUtils.ts. (4) The entire coach Log subsystem is hand-rolled with raw gray-*/blue-*/emerald-* Tailwind literals and bg-blue-600 buttons instead of the Button primitive and design tokens, which the design-system section forbids on coach surfaces. (5) Percentage/RPE prescriptions mislabel the athlete entry cell as "kg" and a one-tap ✓ back-fills the % / RPE number as a kg performed_load — a silent mislog the coach then reviews as truth. (6) Several tap targets fall below the 40px mobile guideline (RAW score buttons h-8=32px, GPP done checkbox 24px, icon buttons p-1≈28px). (7) The "RAW n/12" chip and "Combo" chip render on every applicable row, carrying no signal per the CLAUDE.md chip rule. (8) Custom text metrics are hard-truncated to 14 chars in the only coach review surface. Findings below are evidence-cited and ordered by severity.

findings.title: US date formatting throughout both surfaces violates the non-negotiable European DD/MM, 24h convention
findings.evidence: CLAUDE.md: 'Always use European standards… Dates are day-first (DD/MM/YYYY / DD/MM), never US month-first… Times are 24-hour.' dateUtils.ts already provides formatDateShort (→DD/MM), formatDateToDDMMYYYY and formatDateRange. Yet every date render bypasses them: SessionHeader.tsx:82 uses toLocaleDateString with {month:'short', day:'numeric'} → 'Jun 10' month-first; SessionPreview.tsx:51 same; WeekScreen.tsx:198 same with weekday; WeekNavigator.tsx:39-41 formats the week range as 'Jun 10 – Jun 16' (month-first); LogDayCard.tsx:91 same on the coach side; logFormatUtils.formatTimestamp (line 12-20) produces 'May 20, 2:30 PM' — month-first AND 12-hour AM/PM, used in both LogCommentsThread and AthleteCommentsThread. Because toLocaleDateString(undefined,…) follows the browser locale, a coach on an en-US machine sees fully US dates everywhere.
findings.userImpact: Coaches and athletes in the European OWL context see month-first dates and AM/PM times across the entire training log — date pickers, day headers, week ranges, and comment timestamps — directly contradicting the locale rule the product mandates. Mixed conventions (the date <input> is ISO, the labels are US) make the 'performed on' UI ambiguous.
findings.recommendation: Replace every toLocaleDateString date label with the existing DD/MM helpers (formatDateShort / formatDateToDDMMYYYY / formatDateRange) and rewrite formatTimestamp to DD/MM + HH:mm 24h. Weekday names are fine to keep, but the numeric date must be day-first. This is a mechanical sweep across the six files above.

verdicts.reasoning: Tried to refute on reachability and exact format claims; every claim substantiates against the real code. (1) All six cited lines are verbatim accurate: SessionHeader.tsx:82-86, SessionPreview.tsx:51-55, WeekScreen.tsx:198-201 all call toLocaleDateString(undefined, {weekday, month:'short', day:'numeric'}) → month-first labels like "Jun 10". WeekNavigator.tsx:39-41 formatWeekRange builds "Jun 10 – Jun 16, 2026" (month-first). LogDayCard.tsx:90-94 (coach side) is the same pattern. logFormatUtils.ts:12-20 formatTimestamp uses toLocaleString(undefined, {month:'short', day:'numeric', hour:'2-digit', minute:'2-digit'}) → month-first and, on an en-US locale, 12-hour AM/PM (no hour12 override forces 24h). (2) Every code path is reachable at runtime: SessionHeader is rendered at TodayScreen.tsx:965; SessionPreview at WeekScreen:271, TodayScreen:939, GroupViewerScreen:158; WeekNavigator at WeekScreen:156, TodayScreen:884, GroupViewerScreen:133; LogDayCard at LogModeView.tsx:287 and :319; formatTimestamp is actually imported and rendered in AthleteCommentsThread.tsx:69 and LogCommentsThread.tsx:66. (4) The violation is unconditional: even on a German locale the {month:'short'} option yields localized month names ("10. Juni"), never the mandated DD/MM numeric form, and the AM/PM/24h behaviour is locale-dependent, contradicting the explicit "Times are 24-hour" rule. The finding is, if anything, slightly understated — additional sibling violations exist that it does not list (ProfileScreen.tsx:31, PRDetailScreen.tsx:38/44, CoachThreadScreen.tsx:471/483, TodayScreen.tsx:943). verdicts.correctedEvidence: All six cited file:line locations are correct. Note formatTimestamp's AM/PM is locale-conditional (en-US → 12h, de-DE → 24h) rather than always AM/PM, but it is never guaranteed 24h. Scope is broader than the six listed files: also ProfileScreen.tsx:31, PRDetailScreen.tsx:38/44, CoachThreadScreen.tsx:471/483, TodayScreen.tsx:943 use the same month-first/locale-dependent pattern.

It cannot break any prescription type — purely display formatting. The only requirement to get right: rewrite formatTimestamp to explicit DD/MM + HH:mm 24h (or at minimum pass 'en-GB'), not rely on locale, otherwise a German-locale machine would emit German month names ('20. findings.title: Coach Log subsystem is hand-rolled with raw Tailwind palette + bg-blue-600 buttons instead of design tokens and the Button primitive
findings.evidence: CLAUDE.md design-system section: coach (light) surfaces must use the Button primitive and the CSS design tokens; 'Never hand-roll bg-blue-600/bg-blue-700 buttons'. The coach Log files are pervasively off-token: LogWeekOverview/LogDayCard/LogExerciseRow/CoachSetEditModal use bg-white, bg-gray-50, border-gray-200, text-gray-900/700/500, text-blue-600, text-red-600, bg-amber-50 etc. as raw literals (these can't theme and drift). The 'Send comment' button (LogCommentsThread.tsx:96) is bg-blue-600 hover:bg-blue-500; the 'Add' custom-metric button (WeekMetricsSettings.tsx:440) is bg-blue-600 hover:bg-blue-700 — both the exact off-brand blue (Tailwind #2563EB) the guide calls 'the single most common off-brand tell' vs the brand --color-accent #185FA5. None of these coach buttons use the Button primitive. Notably GroupLogView.tsx (same subsystem) DOES use tokens correctly, proving the divergence is inconsistency, not a constraint.
findings.userImpact: The coach Log mode reads as a 'vibe-coded patchwork' against the rest of the planner: the accent blue is visibly wrong (#2563EB vs brand #185FA5), and it will not respond to the planned dark-mode/accent theming because raw palette literals can't consume tokens. Internally inconsistent with GroupLogView right beside it.
findings.recommendation: Migrate the coach Log components to design tokens per the documented mapping (gray→text/bg/border tokens, blue-600→accent, red→danger, bg-blue-50→accent-muted) and replace the Send/Add/Refresh/Metrics buttons with the Button primitive (primary/secondary/ghost + iconOnly). Leave the data-driven colours (RAW 1-3 bands, status dots, delta green/red) untouched — they encode meaning. This does not touch the athlete dark app (intentionally raw Tailwind).

verdicts.reasoning: Every cited line and condition matches the real code. The two off-brand buttons are verbatim: LogCommentsThread.tsx:96 (Send comment) uses `bg-blue-600 text-white hover:bg-blue-500 disabled:bg-gray-300`, and WeekMetricsSettings.tsx:440 (Add custom metric) uses `bg-blue-600 hover:bg-blue-700 disabled:bg-gray-300 text-white`. A subsystem-wide grep confirms these are the ONLY two bg-blue-600/700/500 buttons — the finding neither over- nor under-states. The pervasive raw-palette claim is confirmed across all cited files: LogWeekOverview.tsx (192,285-291: border-gray-200/bg-white/text-gray-500/900/bg-gray-50), LogDayCard.tsx (99,102,112-130: bg-white/bg-gray-50/text-gray-900/700/500/400, bg-amber-50, text-red-600), LogExerciseRow.tsx (131,136,143,153: text-gray-*/text-blue-600/hover:text-red-600), CoachSetEditModal.tsx (154,171-203,316,332: bg-white border-gray-200, bg-blue-50 border-blue-200 text-blue-500, focus:border-blue-500), LogCommentsThread.tsx (28-29,66,69,90). The comparison claim is verified objectively: GroupLogView.tsx is the SOLE file in src/components/planner/log/ using var(--color-*) tokens — a grep found 17 occurrences, all 17 in that one file, zero elsewhere — proving the divergence is inconsistency, not a constraint. A grep for any Button import from /ui across the whole subsystem returned no matches, confirming every button is hand-rolled. Reachability is solid: these are the shipped/live coach Log mode components per CLAUDE.md; the Send composer renders when a session thread opens (LogDayCard:237-243), the Add button when the metrics form toggles, and the StatCells/DayCards are the primary review surface. I lowered severity from high to medium: this is a real, documented design-system violation (CLAUDE.md explicitly forbids hand-rolled bg-blue-600 buttons and mandates the Button primitive + tokens) with genuine cosmetic/off-brand and theming impact, but it does not affect functionality, data integrity, or correctness — so it is below behavior-affecting bugs in priority. (Tangential: LogDayCard.tsx:90-93 also uses a US-style toLocaleDateString with month:'short' that violates the European DD/MM convention — a separate finding, not part of this one.)

findings.title: Set-vs-accept distinction is not visually signalled — a free-text accept row looks identical to a quantified set row
findings.evidence: A free_text/'other' exercise renders a single freeTextMode SetEntryRow (ExerciseLogCard.tsx:125-135). That row reuses the exact same chrome as a numeric set: the same row container, the same set-number badge (SetEntryRow.tsx:193), and the same 8×8 ✓ / ✗ pills (lines 199-224). The only difference is the merged prose cell vs two numeric cells. There is no label, icon, or heading telling the athlete 'this exercise is accept-only, there is nothing to measure' versus 'log your kg×reps here'. The set-number '1' badge on a single accept row is actively misleading — it implies 'set 1 of N'.
findings.userImpact: Athletes can't tell whether they're being asked to record numbers or simply confirm they did something. The set-number on an accept-only row suggests more sets exist. This is the conceptual confusion behind the user's report that the log 'feels buggy'.
findings.recommendation: Give the accept-only row a distinct, signal-bearing treatment: drop the numeric set-number badge, and label the row 'Mark done' / 'Did you do this?' rather than reusing the set chrome. Reserve the numbered ✓/kg/reps row strictly for quantified sets, so the two modes are instantly distinguishable.

verdicts.reasoning:
Re-read both cited files. The code path is reachable: a coach authoring unit='free_text' or 'other' produces exactly one synthesised row at ExerciseLogCard.tsx:125-135 (setNumber:1, freeTextMode:true, freeTextPlanned:prescription_raw). That row is rendered by SetEntryRow, which shares all chrome with quantified rows:
- The set-number badge at SetEntryRow.tsx:193-195 (`{input.setNumber}` → "1") renders UNCONDITIONALLY, before the freeTextMode branch — confirmed identical for accept rows.
- The 8×8 ✓/✗ pills at SetEntryRow.tsx:197-226 (w-8 h-8 buttons) render unconditionally (only gated on !readOnly) — confirmed identical.
- The ONLY divergence is the freeTextMode ternary at line 228: a merged prose/text cell (lines 229-251) vs two NumericCells (lines 253-275). No label, icon, or heading signals "accept-only / nothing to measure". So the structural claim is accurate and the misleading "1" badge on a single accept row genuinely renders.
Honest caveats that keep this 'medium' rather than higher: (1) there IS one minor visual difference the evidence understates — the planned free-text cell renders the coach's prose in italic (line 231, `text-sm italic`), so the row isn't pixel-identical to a numeric row; the prose-vs-numeric-fields contrast is itself a soft signal. (2) For an EXTRA (athlete-added) free-text row the cell is an editable input with placeholder 'What did you do?' (line 246), which is somewhat self-explanatory. findings.title: CoachSetEditModal exposes only kg/reps inputs for prose/percentage/RPE exercises and silently drops performed_text on commit
findings.evidence: EditableRow (CoachSetEditModal.tsx:266-344) always renders a kg input + reps input regardless of the exercise unit. commit() (lines 251-257) writes only performed_load/performed_reps via parseNumericInput; it never reads or writes performed_text. upsertLoggedSet in saveRow (lines 106-115) likewise omits performedText. So when a coach opens the edit modal on a free_text exercise (whose set carries performed_text prose and null numerics) or a combo (whose performed_text holds '2+2+2'), the modal shows empty kg/reps boxes; the moment the coach touches a field and it commits, the row is re-saved with performed_text unchanged at the DB only because it isn't in the patch — but the modal's own display shows nothing, and adding any value converts the prose set into a numeric one with no way back. The read-only Prescription header (lines 170-181) also renders for percentage/RPE where per-set kg editing is conceptually wrong.
findings.userImpact: A coach correcting a logged session for a free-text or combo exercise sees blank fields (the athlete's prose '2+2+2' or 'felt easy' is invisible) and can inadvertently overwrite it with a kg/reps interpretation. The coach's corrected log no longer reflects what the athlete reported.
findings.recommendation: Make CoachSetEditModal unit-aware: for free_text/'other' show a text field bound to performed_text and hide kg/reps; for combos show the tuple string; for percentage/RPE label the cell accordingly. At minimum, surface existing performed_text so the coach never edits blind.

verdicts.reasoning:
Reachable and real, with worse data loss than the finding describes.

Reachability: LogExerciseRow (src/components/planner/log/LogExerciseRow.tsx) routes only sentinels (TEXT/IMAGE/VIDEO → SentinelDisplay; GPP → its own editor) away from the modal. Every non-sentinel unit — free_text, free_text_reps, percentage, rpe, and is_combo — falls through to the standard row (line 220+) and renders the edit pencil for ANY logged exercise (line 257: `logged && onEdit`). LogDayCard wires onEdit→onEditLoggedExercise(ledg) for both planned (line 179) and off-plan (line 208) logged rows unconditionally, and LogModeView passes editingLogged.sets straight into CoachSetEditModal (lines 335-349). So a free-text or combo exercise the athlete logged is editable in this modal. The athlete app demonstrably populates performed_text for these: SetEntryRow free-text mode writes performedText (SetEntryRow.tsx:125) and combo "2+2+2" entries write performedText (SetEntryRow.tsx:147-148), persisted via training_log_sets.performed_text (migration 20260520000003).

The cited lines are accurate: EditableRow only reads/edits performed_load + performed_reps (CoachSetEditModal.tsx:238-239, 303-333); commit() (251-257) emits only performed_load/performed_reps; saveRow's SetPatch (104-115) omits performedText.

The finding's data-loss reasoning is actually too mild. It claims performed_text "survives at the DB only because it isn't in the patch." That is wrong in the safe direction. upsertLoggedSet (trainingLogService.ts:725-747) does `.upsert(row, { onConflict: 'log_exercise_id,set_number' })` where `row.performed_text = patch.performedText ?? null` (line 733). Since the coach modal never sets performedText, this resolves to null, and the ON CONFLICT DO UPDATE replaces every column on the existing row — so performed_text is actively WIPED to null, not preserved. Worse, this isn't limited to editing a numeric field: cycleDone/cycleSkipped (CoachSetEditModal.tsx:259-264) also call onSave→saveRow→upsertLoggedSet, so a coach merely toggling the ✓/✗ status on a combo/free-text set silently destroys the athlete's "2+2+2" or prose value.

Net effect: a coach reviewing/correcting a free-text or combo logged session sees blank kg/reps boxes (the athlete's reported value is invisible), and any interaction — typing or even flipping done/skip — overwrites performed_text to null at the DB. This is a genuine, easily-triggered data-loss and review-integrity bug, so I raise severity from medium to high. (The header note that the read-only Prescription block renders for percentage/RPE is correct but secondary/cosmetic.)
verdicts.correctedEvidence: Confirmed mechanism is data destruction, not silent preservation: upsertLoggedSet (src/lib/trainingLogService.ts:733) sets performed_text = patch.performedText ?? null and upserts on conflict ('log_exercise_id,set_number'), so the coach modal's writes (CoachSetEditModal.tsx:104-115) replace performed_text with null. Triggered by editing a field (commit, lines 251-257) AND by toggling status (cycleDone/cycleSkipped, lines 259-264). Reachability confirmed via LogExerciseRow.tsx:257 + LogDayCard.tsx:179,208 + LogModeView.tsx:335-349; athlete writes performed_text at SetEntryRow.tsx:125 and 147-148.

findings.title: Touch targets below the 40px mobile guideline: RAW score buttons (32px), GPP done checkbox (24px), icon buttons (~28px)
findings.evidence: The athlete app is a mobile-first touch surface, so the ~44px Apple / 48px Material minimum tap target applies. RAW score buttons are h-8 (32px tall) in a 3-col grid (RawScoreDial.tsx:181) — the primary readiness input the coach asked for. The GPP per-row 'Done' checkbox is w-6 h-6 = 24px (GppLogCard.tsx:166). The Substitute icon button is p-1 around a 12px icon ≈ 28px (ExerciseLogCard.tsx:284-288), and the per-set Trash is p-1.5 around a 13px icon ≈ 30px (SetEntryRow.tsx:281-285). By contrast the ✓/✗ pills are correctly sized w-8 h-8=32px… still under 40px. The GPP numeric/reps/load inputs (GppLogCard.tsx:184-205) are bare borderless text with focus-only background — a tiny invisible hit area until focused.
findings.userImpact: Athletes log in the gym, often with chalked or sweaty hands on a phone. 24-32px targets cause mis-taps — wrong RAW pillar selected, wrong GPP row ticked, accidental set deletion — degrading the core logging task.
findings.recommendation: Raise the primary interactive targets toward ~40px: make RAW score buttons at least h-10, the GPP done checkbox w-9 h-9 (or wrap the whole cell as the hit area), and pad the Substitute/Trash icon buttons to a 36-40px hit box (visual icon can stay small). Give the GPP table inputs a persistent subtle border so the editable area is discoverable.

verdicts.reasoning:
I read all four cited files and verified each measurement against the actual Tailwind classes; the components are reachable in the live athlete logging flow (all four are imported/used under src/athlete/v2, with RawScoreDial, GppLogCard, ExerciseLogCard rendered from TodayScreen.tsx — the main logging screen — and SetEntryRow consumed by ExerciseLogCard).

Verified facts:
- RawScoreDial.tsx:172-192 — RAW score buttons are h-8 (32px tall) in a grid-cols-3. Confirmed. These are the primary readiness inputs.
- GppLogCard.tsx:164-176 — the per-row "Done" toggle button is w-6 h-6 = 24px. Confirmed, the smallest target.
- GppLogCard.tsx:184-205 — Reps/Sets/Load inputs are bare bg-transparent with focus:bg-gray-800 and no persistent border, i.e. an invisible hit area until focused. Confirmed exactly as described.
- ExerciseLogCard.tsx:278-289 — Substitute icon button is p-1 around a size=12 Replace icon. Confirmed (computed box ~20px, even smaller than the finding's ~28px claim — overstated but in the conservative direction).
- SetEntryRow.tsx:277-287 — per-set Trash is p-1.5 around a size=13 Trash2 icon. Confirmed (computed box ~25px, vs finding's ~30px — again overstated in the conservative direction).
- SetEntryRow.tsx:202,215 — the ✓/✗ status pills are w-8 h-8 = 32px, matching the finding's "correctly sized 32px… still under 40px" note.

CLAUDE.md context explicitly states the athlete app's dark Tailwind colours are intentional and must not be flagged — but this finding is about touch-target SIZE / a11y, not colour tokens, so it is not exempted by that rule. The athlete app is a mobile-first gym surface, so the ~44px Apple / 48px Material tap-target guideline is the right lens; 24-32px targets are genuinely below it.

The only inaccuracies are the two icon-button pixel estimates (~28px / ~30px), which actually compute smaller (~20px / ~25px), so the underlying problem is real and if anything slightly worse than stated. That is a minor evidence imprecision, not a refutation, so verdict is confirmed rather than partial. Medium severity is appropriate: these are real mis-tap risks on the core logging task (wrong RAW pillar, wrong GPP row, accidental set deletion), but they degrade rather than block the flow.
verdicts.correctedEvidence: Icon-button hit boxes are even smaller than stated: Substitute is p-1 around a 12px icon ≈ 20px box (ExerciseLogCard.tsx:284-288); Trash is p-1.5 around a 13px icon ≈ 25px box (SetEntryRow.tsx:281-285). All other measurements (RAW h-8=32px, GPP done w-6 h-6=24px, ✓/✗ pills w-8 h-8=32px, borderless GPP inputs) are exact.
