findings.title: Always-present chips carry no signal — 'RAW n/12', 'Combo', and 'GPP' badges appear on every applicable row
findings.evidence: CLAUDE.md chip rule: 'render a chip only when it conveys actionable, non-obvious information. A chip that appears on every row carries no signal — drop it. Prefer a title tooltip for terse/jargon labels (e.g. a RAW …/12 score).' The 'RAW n/12' chip renders on every logged day header (LogDayCard.tsx:129-131) and every SessionPreview (SessionPreview.tsx:93-95) with no explanation of the 4-12 scale. The 'Combo' chip (ExerciseLogCard.tsx:273; SessionPreview.tsx:257; LogExerciseRow.tsx:244) repeats on every combo row even though the member-dot list directly below already shows it's a combo. The 'GPP' badge (GppLogCard.tsx:130) repeats on every GPP block.
findings.userImpact: Visual noise that dilutes the genuinely useful chips (the substitution '⇄ for X' and 'Added by athlete'). The unexplained 'RAW /12' jargon has no tooltip, so a coach onboarding doesn't know what 12 means.
findings.recommendation: For RAW, keep the number but add a title tooltip explaining the 4-12 Eleiko scale (or colour-band it) rather than a bare chip. For 'Combo', drop the chip when the member-dot row is already shown, or keep only one of the two redundant signals. These are nits but they're explicit CLAUDE.md guidance.

verdicts.reasoning: All cited lines render as described, but the finding conflates two distinct UI elements and slightly overstates the "every row" framing. RAW: At LogDayCard.tsx:129-131 and SessionPreview.tsx:93-95 the markup is `RAW {session.raw_total}/12` with NO title attribute — the "unexplained /12 jargon, no tooltip" claim is factually correct. However this is NOT a chip/badge: it has no background, no rounded pill, and sits inline alongside BW/duration/comment count as a session-metric strip. It is also gated on `session.raw_total != null`, so it appears only when a RAW score was actually logged, not "on every row." The CLAUDE.md chip rule the finding invokes is about pill badges; applying it to an inline metric label is a stretch. The tooltip suggestion is reasonable but minor. Combo: Confirmed redundant in the athlete components. ExerciseLogCard.tsx:273-277 renders the "Combo" badge and ExerciseLogCard.tsx:292-294 renders the member-dot+name list directly below (same for SessionPreview.tsx:256-260 + 264). So in those two views the badge is genuinely duplicated by an adjacent, more-informative member list. In the coach view LogExerciseRow.tsx:244-248 the "Combo" badge is present but I did not confirm an adjacent member-dot row in that component, so the redundancy is strongest in the two athlete components. GPP: Confirmed at GppLogCard.tsx:130-132 — the "GPP" pill renders on every GPP block unconditionally. This is the clearest always-present-no-signal case, since the card is already structurally a GPP block. Net: the underlying observations are real (no RAW tooltip; redundant Combo badge next to member list; ever-present GPP pill), so not refuted — but the "appears on every row / chip" characterization is imprecise (RAW is a conditional inline metric, not a chip; Combo redundancy is per-exercise not per-row). These are explicit-but-trivial CLAUDE.md nits with no functional impact, so nit rather than low.
verdicts.correctedEvidence: RAW is an inline session-metric label (no background/pill), gated on session.raw_total != null, at LogDayCard.tsx:129-131 and SessionPreview.tsx:93-95 — correct that it has no title tooltip. Combo badge at ExerciseLogCard.tsx:273-277 and SessionPreview.tsx:256-260 is redundant with the adjacent member-dot list (ExerciseLogCard.tsx:292-294, SessionPreview.tsx:264-274); LogExerciseRow.tsx:244-248 also renders it. GPP pill is unconditional at GppLogCard.tsx:130-132.

findings.title: Custom text-metric values hard-truncated to 14 chars in the only coach review surface; numeric avg uses period decimals not EU comma
findings.evidence: OtherMetricsTable renders text custom metrics as String(v).slice(0,14) (LogWeekOverview.tsx:588) with the full value only in a cell title tooltip (line 578). A coach-defined 'Pain area' text metric like 'Lower right SI joint' shows as 'Lower right SI' inline. There is no other coach surface for custom metric values (not on the LogDayCard glance, not in SessionPreview). Separately, numeric formatting uses toFixed(1) / Number.isInteger checks (lines 221, 592, 584) producing '82.5' with a period, while CLAUDE.md mandates comma decimals for user-facing numbers (the athlete BodyweightField correctly accepts comma input, BodyweightField.tsx:21).
findings.userImpact: A coach scanning the week table for a wellbeing flag they explicitly created can't read the value without hovering each cell — defeating the at-a-glance purpose. Period decimals are inconsistent with the comma-decimal convention used on input.
findings.recommendation: Allow text metric cells to wrap or expand (the table already scrolls-x), or render them in a stacked single-column-per-day layout. Apply a shared comma-decimal formatter to the numeric metric/avg cells to match the project convention.

verdicts.reasoning:
All cited conditions verified against the real code and confirmed reachable.

(1) Hard 14-char truncation: LogWeekOverview.tsx:588 renders text custom metrics as String(v).slice(0,14). This path is genuinely reachable: a coach defines a text metric (value_type==='text'), the athlete enters free text via a <textarea> with no length cap (CustomMetricField.tsx:62-70, commits value_text on blur), the value is stored in session.custom_metrics[def.id] and read back at LogWeekOverview.tsx:516-521 into a non-numeric row (numeric=false at line 514/529). A value like 'Lower right SI joint' (20 chars) is cut to 'Lower right SI'. The full value appears only in the cell title tooltip (LogWeekOverview.tsx:578), so a coach scanning the week table cannot read it without hovering each cell.

(2) Only coach surface: confirmed. Custom-metric VALUE display across src is limited to LogWeekOverview.tsx among coach surfaces. LogDayCard (the coach glance) shows only BW/RAW/duration/comments (LogDayCard.tsx:124-140) — no custom metrics or VAS. SessionPreview.tsx and SessionHeader.tsx are athlete-app files (src/athlete/v2/*), not coach review. WeekMetricsSettings.tsx is the definition editor, not a value display.

(3) Period-decimal formatting: confirmed at every cited line — cell numeric branch LogWeekOverview.tsx:584-586 (Number.isInteger(v) ? v : v.toFixed(1)), avg cell line 592 (a.toFixed(1)), and Avg kg/rep StatCells lines 221-222 (performedAvg.toFixed(1) / plannedAvg.toFixed(1)). These produce '82.5' with a period, contrary to CLAUDE.md's comma-decimal mandate for user-facing numbers. The athlete side accepts comma input (BodyweightField.tsx:21-22 replace(',','.'); CustomMetricField.tsx:30 same), so input and coach-review display are inconsistent.

Severity stays low and is honest: both issues are real but cosmetic/scan-cost, not data corruption. Note the period-decimal issue is actually broader than the finding frames it (toFixed periods are pervasive in the light coach app, e.g. LogDayCard.tsx:127), so the comma-decimal point is a project-wide convention gap rather than localized to OtherMetricsTable — but that does not weaken the finding; it strengthens it. The 14-char truncation is the genuinely localized, novel defect. Both confirmed.

findings.title: Sentinel notes (TEXT/IMAGE/VIDEO) count as unloggable cards with no acknowledge affordance — sessions read 'incomplete'
findings.evidence: TEXT/IMAGE/VIDEO sentinels early-return a SentinelDisplay note card + a notes textarea, with NO ✓/✗ and no done state (ExerciseLogCard.tsx:179-204). expectedPlannedSetCount returns null for them so they're excluded from auto-promote, but they're still rendered as full cards in the day list and contribute to the perceived 'X/Y exercises' picture. There's no way for the athlete to dismiss/acknowledge 'I read the coach's note' or 'I watched the video'.
findings.userImpact: An athlete who has done everything still sees informational cards sitting in the list with no completion state, and a coach reviewing can't tell if the athlete actually read the note/watched the video. Minor, but it adds to the 'feels unfinished' impression.
findings.recommendation: Consider an optional lightweight 'Got it' acknowledge toggle on sentinel cards (persisted on performed_notes/a flag) so informational blocks have a terminal state, or visually de-emphasise them (e.g. collapse by default) so they don't read as outstanding work. This is a product decision — flag for the synthesizer, not a forced change.

verdicts.reasoning:
Core observation is code-accurate; the impact mechanism is overstated and one cited path is wrong.

CONFIRMED parts: In src/athlete/v2/components/ExerciseLogCard.tsx the TEXT/IMAGE/VIDEO sentinel branch (line 179, `if (sentinelType === 'text' || 'image' || 'video')`) early-returns at lines 180-203 a SentinelDisplay note card plus a free notes textarea, with NO ✓/✗, no done/complete state, and no acknowledge affordance. There is genuinely no terminal "I read this / watched this" state for informational sentinel cards. expectedPlannedSetCount returns null for sentinels (src/lib/plannedSetCount.ts:29) and the auto-promote sweep in TodayScreen.tsx:557 bails on a null count, so they are correctly excluded from auto-completion — matches the finding.

REFUTED parts (why partial, not confirmed):
1. Wrong file path: the finding cites src/athlete/v2/components/TodayScreen.tsx:321, but the screen lives at src/athlete/v2/screens/TodayScreen.tsx. Line 321 there is `exercises: []` inside mergeSession — unrelated to any completion picture.
2. The claimed harm ("contribute to the perceived 'X/Y exercises' picture", sessions "read incomplete") is NOT substantiated by the code. I searched every day/session surface: DayChipRow.tsx:26 and SessionPreview.tsx:57/85 compute day status as a BINARY `session.status === 'completed'`, not an exercise count — sentinels feed into no percentage or X/Y aggregate. The ONLY "X/Y" counter in the app is the per-card `{completedCount}/{rows.length || '—'}` at ExerciseLogCard.tsx:321, and that JSX is unreachable for sentinel cards because the sentinel branch already returned at line 180. So a sentinel card renders no counter and contributes to no count.
3. Session completion is athlete-driven via an explicit "Finish session" button (TodayScreen.tsx:1041-1048), not a derived completed-count, so an un-acknowledged sentinel can never hold a session in an "incomplete" state.

Net: a real, minor product gap (informational cards lack a terminal/acknowledge state, and a coach can't tell if a note was read), but the severity-justifying impact in the finding is overstated. Downgrade low -> nit; legitimately a product decision for the synthesizer, not a bug.
verdicts.correctedEvidence: src/athlete/v2/components/ExerciseLogCard.tsx:179-204 (sentinel TEXT/IMAGE/VIDEO branch: SentinelDisplay + notes textarea, no ✓/✗ or acknowledge state). Correct screen path is src/athlete/v2/screens/TodayScreen.tsx (not components/). Note: there is NO session-level "X/Y exercises" counter — day/session status is binary (session.status==='completed' at SessionPreview.tsx:57/85, DayChipRow.tsx:26), and the only per-card X/Y counter (ExerciseLogCard.tsx:321) is unreachable for sentinels due to the early return at line 180, so the "reads incomplete" rationale does not hold.

findings.dimensionOverview: The two surfaces are well-built in many respects: the athlete dark theme is internally consistent, set-entry has good optimistic-save discipline, destructive single-set deletes route through an UndoToast and bulk/irreversible deletes through a ConfirmModal (TodayScreen.tsx), and the coach Log review reuses the canonical StackedNotation so plan/did read coherently. The shared StackedNotation/SentinelDisplay and the GroupLogView (LogModeView surfaces) correctly use design tokens. However there are several reachable UX defects that violate stated EMOS rules. (1) The user's headline concern is real and visually under-served: free_text_reps produces kg-labelled numeric SetEntryRows (load cell unit="kg") even though the planned "load" is prose, and a free_text_reps prescription with no parsable "× reps" yields a dead "No set lines defined" card with no way to accept/skip — the athlete cannot log it. (2) The set-vs-accept distinction is NOT visually signalled: a free_text exercise renders the same ✓/✗ pill chrome and the same card as a quantified set, so the athlete/coach can't tell "this is an accept-only note" from "this is a real set" at a glance. (3) European date conventions (a non-negotiable CLAUDE.md rule) are violated in EVERY date render on both surfaces — SessionHeader, SessionPreview, WeekScreen, WeekNavigator, LogDayCard and logFormatUtils all call toLocaleDateString with US-style month-first options or h:mm AM/PM, bypassing the existing DD/MM helpers in dateUtils.ts. (4) The entire coach Log subsystem is hand-rolled with raw gray-*/blue-*/emerald-* Tailwind literals and bg-blue-600 buttons instead of the Button primitive and design tokens, which the design-system section forbids on coach surfaces. (5) Percentage/RPE prescriptions mislabel the athlete entry cell as "kg" and a one-tap ✓ back-fills the % / RPE number as a kg performed_load — a silent mislog the coach then reviews as truth. (6) Several tap targets fall below the 40px mobile guideline (RAW score buttons h-8=32px, GPP done checkbox 24px, icon buttons p-1≈28px). (7) The "RAW n/12" chip and "Combo" chip render on every applicable row, carrying no signal per the CLAUDE.md chip rule. (8) Custom text metrics are hard-truncated to 14 chars in the only coach review surface. Findings below are evidence-cited and ordered by severity.

findings.title: Inconsistent slot/day labelling: 'Day {dayIndex}' vs 'Day {idx + 1}' across athlete screens
findings.evidence: TodayScreen falls back to `Day ${dayIndex}` (lines 940, 967) when no coach label exists, while GroupViewerScreen falls back to `Day ${idx + 1}` (line 81). For the same underlying slot, the group viewer and the today screen would print different day numbers (e.g. 'Day 0' vs 'Day 1'), since dayIndex is 0-based in some paths. WeekScreen meanwhile prints the raw dayIndex as a large number badge (WeekScreen.tsx:224-226).
findings.userImpact: An athlete switching between the week-at-a-glance, the group viewer, and the today editor can see the same training slot labelled with different numbers, which undermines trust that they're looking at the same day.
findings.recommendation: Centralise the default day label into one helper (e.g. `defaultSlotLabel(dayIndex)`), pick one numbering base, and use it everywhere a coach label is absent.

verdicts.reasoning: The core inconsistency is real and reachable. For an unlabeled slot with dayIndex=N: GroupViewerScreen.tsx:81 falls back to `Day ${idx + 1}`, while TodayScreen.tsx:940 and :967 fall back to `Day ${dayIndex}`. The canonical helper is DEFAULT_LABEL = (i) => `Day ${i}` at src/lib/trainingLogService.ts:354, used by fetchWeekOverview (line 439/454) which feeds both WeekScreen (raw badge `day.dayIndex` at WeekScreen.tsx:225) and TodayScreen. So GroupViewerScreen is the lone outlier that adds +1, and its number will be off by exactly 1 from every other surface for the same slot. The recommendation (centralise into one helper, one base) is sound — DEFAULT_LABEL already exists and GroupViewerScreen simply doesn't reuse it.\n\nTwo parts of the evidence are wrong, hence partial: (1) The claim that "dayIndex is 0-based in some paths" producing "Day 0 vs Day 1" is incorrect. The data model is firmly 1-based: WeeklyPlanner.tsx:176 defaults active_days to [1,2,3,4,5]; the day-config rebuild loops `for (let i = 1; i <= maxDay; i++)` (WeeklyPlanner.tsx:286, :1024); PrintWeek.tsx:173 and PrintWeekDesigner.tsx:319 default to [1,2,3,4,5,6,7]. day_index never equals 0 for a normal slot, so the real mismatch for a default slot is "Day 1" (Today/Week) vs "Day 2" (GroupViewer), not "Day 0 vs Day 1". (2) The surface/userImpact is mischaracterized: GroupViewerScreen.tsx is a coach/group-viewer screen — it authenticates via `group` and queries week_plans WHERE athlete_id IS NULL (GroupViewerScreen.tsx:33-34, 49). An athlete does not navigate to it, so the "athlete switching between week-at-a-glance, group viewer, and today editor" scenario doesn't apply to an athlete; the inconsistency surfaces to a coach comparing the group viewer against the per-athlete Today/Week numbering. Nit severity stands — purely a cosmetic numbering inconsistency on an unlabeled-slot fallback, with no functional impact.
verdicts.correctedEvidence: GroupViewerScreen.tsx:81 uses `labels[idx] || `Day ${idx + 1}``, whereas TodayScreen.tsx:940 & :967 use `selectedOverviewDay?.label ?? `Day ${dayIndex}`` and the canonical fallback is DEFAULT_LABEL = (i) => `Day ${i}` at src/lib/trainingLogService.ts:354 (consumed by WeekScreen via overview labels and by the raw badge at WeekScreen.tsx:225). day_index is 1-based (active_days defaults [1,2,3,4,5] at WeeklyPlanner.tsx:176), so for an unlabeled slot dayIndex=1 the GroupViewer prints "Day 2" while Today/Week print "Day 1" — off by one, not "Day 0 vs Day 1". GroupViewerScreen is a coach group-viewer (athlete_id IS NULL query), not an athlete screen.

findings.title: Substitution applies instantly with no confirmation and silently inherits the original exercise's planned kg/% set lines
findings.evidence: Tapping the Replace icon opens the ExercisePicker; onPick immediately calls handleSubstitute (TodayScreen.tsx:1092-1094) with no confirm step, even though other destructive-ish actions (remove planned set, delete off-plan) go through ConfirmModal. After substitution the set rows still come from the ORIGINAL planned slot's set lines (ExerciseLogCard rows memo reads planned.setLines, lines 94-140) — so substituting Snatch (80kg×3) → Power Snatch leaves the athlete with the Snatch's 80kg×3 prescription rows attached to a different lift. The purple '⇄ for X' chip surfaces the swap, but the inherited loads are not flagged as 'from the original exercise'.
findings.userImpact: An athlete can mis-tap into a substitution with no undo prompt, and then logs against load/rep targets that belong to a different lift, which the coach reviews as if prescribed for the substitute. Lower severity because the swap itself is clearly labelled.
findings.recommendation: Keep substitution lightweight but make the inherited prescription explicit — e.g. a one-line 'Targets carried over from {planned}' under the Plan row — and consider an undo affordance consistent with the rest of the app's destructive-action pattern. This is a product judgement; surface to the synthesizer.

verdicts.reasoning: All three sub-claims are substantiated by the real code, and the path is reachable at runtime via the Replace icon on any non-readonly planned exercise card. (1) No confirmation: the substitution ExercisePicker's onPick (TodayScreen.tsx:1092-1094) calls handleSubstitute immediately with no ConfirmModal, whereas the adjacent destructive actions handleRemovePlannedSet (TodayScreen.tsx:719-737) and handleDeleteOffPlanExercise (TodayScreen.tsx:739-762) both route through setPendingConfirm/ConfirmModal — so the inconsistency premise is real. (2) Inherited set lines: the rows memo in ExerciseLogCard (lines 94-140) is keyed on planned.setLines / planned.exercise.unit / prescription_raw, i.e. the ORIGINAL planned slot's data, and the card is fed planned={p} from data.planned (TodayScreen.tsx:1000-1002). setSubstitutedExercise (trainingLogService.ts:762-774) only UPDATEs training_log_exercises.exercise_id — it never touches planned_exercises or set lines — so after a Snatch(80kg×3)→Power Snatch swap the athlete logs against the Snatch's set rows. (3) Not flagged as 'from the original': the only surfacing is the purple chip '⇄ for {plannedName}' with a title tooltip (ExerciseLogCard.tsx:261-267); the StackedNotation/Plan row and set rows carry no 'targets carried over from {planned}' note. Honest caveats keeping this at low: the swap IS visibly labelled (chip + tooltip + the card title switches to the performed exercise name at lines 256-259), and no planned data is mutated (EMOS data-integrity principle 4 is intact — the original prescription stays read-only), and the action is reversible by substituting back. So this is a soft UX best-practice gap (missing confirm/undo affordance + unlabelled inherited targets), not a data-loss bug. Matches the reviewer's stated low severity and 'product judgement — surface to synthesizer' framing.
